questions.stem: Under ASC 715, how is the funded status of a defined benefit pension plan reported on the employer's balance sheet?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The difference between plan assets and the accumulated benefit obligation (ABO)

choices.label: B
choices.text: The difference between the fair value of plan assets and the projected benefit obligation (PBO)

choices.label: C
choices.text: Only the current year's pension expense

choices.label: D
choices.text: The vested benefit obligation

questions.explanation: ASC 715-20-45-1 requires employers to recognize the funded status of a defined benefit plan as an asset or liability. Funded status = Fair value of plan assets - Projected benefit obligation (PBO). If PBO > assets, a liability is recognized; if assets > PBO, an asset is recognized.
questions.explanationJa: 確定給付制度の積立状況（funded status）は、制度資産の公正価値 - PBO で算出し、資産または負債として貸借対照表に認識する。PBO > 資産なら負債、資産 > PBO なら資産となる。

questions.stem: Which of the following is NOT a component of net periodic pension cost?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Service cost

choices.label: B
choices.text: Interest cost on the PBO

choices.label: C
choices.text: Expected return on plan assets

choices.label: D
choices.text: Contributions made by the employer to the plan

questions.explanation: The components of net periodic pension cost are: (1) service cost, (2) interest cost, (3) expected return on plan assets (reduces cost), (4) amortization of prior service cost, (5) amortization of net gain or loss, and (6) amortization of transition obligation. Employer contributions are cash flows that fund the plan but are not a component of pension expense.
questions.explanationJa: 正味期間年金費用（NPPC）の構成要素は次の6つである：(1) 勤務費用、(2) 利息費用、(3) 制度資産の期待収益（費用を減少）、(4) 過去勤務費用の償却、(5) 純損益の償却、(6) 移行時債務の償却。雇用主の拠出金は制度への資金供給であり、年金費用の構成要素ではない。

questions.stem: Under ASU 2017-07, where should the service cost component of net periodic pension cost be presented in the income statement?

choices.label: A
choices.text: All components are presented together in operating expenses

choices.label: B
choices.text: Service cost in operating expenses; all other components outside of operating income

choices.label: C
choices.text: All components are presented in other comprehensive income

choices.label: D
choices.text: Service cost in COGS; other components in SG&A

questions.explanation: ASU 2017-07 (ASC 715-30-45-3A) requires that only the service cost component be presented in the same income statement line as other employee compensation costs (operating expenses). All other components (interest cost, expected return, amortizations) must be presented separately from service cost and outside of operating income.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2017-07により、勤務費用のみを他の従業員報酬費用と同じ行（営業費用）に表示する。その他の構成要素（利息費用、期待収益、償却額）は勤務費用と分離し、営業利益の外に表示しなければならない。

questions.stem: When a pension plan is amended to provide increased benefits for past service, the resulting prior service cost is:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Recognized immediately in pension expense

choices.label: B
choices.text: Recognized in other comprehensive income (OCI) and amortized to expense over the remaining service period of active employees

choices.label: C
choices.text: Charged directly to retained earnings

choices.label: D
choices.text: Added to goodwill

questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-20-35-24, prior service cost arising from a plan amendment is initially recognized in OCI. It is then amortized from accumulated OCI to net periodic pension cost over the future service periods of active employees expected to receive benefits. The amortization typically uses a straight-line method over the average remaining service period.
questions.explanationJa: 制度改定による過去勤務費用は、まずOCIに認識する。その後、給付を受ける見込みの現役従業員の将来勤務期間にわたり、累積OCIからNPPCへ償却する。償却は通常、平均残存勤務期間にわたる定額法を用いる。

questions.stem: Under the corridor approach for amortizing net gains/losses, amortization is required when the unrecognized net gain or loss exceeds:

choices.label: A
choices.text: 5% of the greater of PBO or plan assets

choices.label: B
choices.text: 10% of the greater of PBO or market-related value of plan assets

choices.label: C
choices.text: 15% of the PBO

choices.label: D
choices.text: 20% of plan assets

questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-30-35-24 (the corridor approach), amortization of the net gain or loss is required only if the cumulative unrecognized net gain or loss exceeds 10% of the greater of the PBO or the market-related value of plan assets at the beginning of the year. The excess is amortized over the average remaining service period of active employees.
questions.explanationJa: 回廊アプローチでは、累積未認識純損益がPBOまたは制度資産の市場関連価値のいずれか大きい方の10%を超える場合にのみ償却が必要となる。超過額を現役従業員の平均残存勤務期間で除した金額が最低償却額である。

questions.stem: What is the primary difference between the projected benefit obligation (PBO) and the accumulated benefit obligation (ABO)?

choices.label: A
choices.text: PBO includes assumed future salary increases; ABO does not

choices.label: B
choices.text: ABO includes future salary increases; PBO does not

choices.label: C
choices.text: PBO uses a higher discount rate

choices.label: D
choices.text: There is no difference; they are synonymous

questions.explanation: The PBO measures the obligation using projected (expected future) salary levels, while the ABO measures the obligation based on current salary levels only. The PBO is used for pension expense computation and balance sheet recognition. The ABO is used for the additional minimum liability disclosure requirement.
questions.explanationJa: PBO（予測給付債務）は将来の予測給与水準を用いて債務を測定するのに対し、ABO（累積給付債務）は現在の給与水準のみで測定する。PBOは年金費用の計算と貸借対照表の認識に使用し、ABOは追加最低負債の開示に使用する。

questions.stem: A plan curtailment occurs when an event significantly reduces the expected years of future service of active employees. The effect of a curtailment on prior service cost is:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Ignored until employees actually leave

choices.label: B
choices.text: Recognized immediately in earnings for the portion of prior service cost related to the reduced future service

choices.label: C
choices.text: Deferred and amortized over 10 years

choices.label: D
choices.text: Recognized in OCI only

questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-30-35-92, a curtailment results in immediate recognition in earnings of the portion of prior service cost (from accumulated OCI) associated with the years of future service no longer expected to be rendered. Additionally, any curtailment gain or loss from the change in PBO is recognized.
questions.explanationJa: 縮小（カーテイルメント）が発生すると、今後提供されなくなる将来勤務年数に対応する過去勤務費用（累積OCIから）を損益として即時認識する。さらにPBOの変動による縮小損益も認識する。

questions.stem: Other postretirement employee benefits (OPEB), such as retiree health care, differ from pensions in that:

choices.label: A
choices.text: OPEB plans are always funded; pensions are not

choices.label: B
choices.text: OPEB plans are generally not funded; the obligation is called the APBO (accumulated postretirement benefit obligation)

choices.label: C
choices.text: OPEB uses the same accounting as pensions with no differences

choices.label: D
choices.text: OPEB obligations are never reported on the balance sheet

questions.explanation: OPEB plans (ASC 715-60) are generally unfunded (no plan assets), unlike pension plans which typically have a trust holding plan assets. The obligation is measured as the APBO (accumulated postretirement benefit obligation). The general accounting framework is similar to pensions (recognize funded status, OCI items), but the lack of funding makes the liability typically larger relative to assets.
questions.explanationJa: OPEB制度は通常、制度資産を持たない（未積立）。これは信託に資産を保有する年金制度との大きな違いである。債務はAPBO（累積退職後給付債務）として測定する。会計の枠組みは年金と類似するが（積立状況の認識、OCI項目）、積立がないため負債が大きくなりやすい。

questions.stem: A pension plan has plan assets with a fair value of $2,000,000 and uses a market-related value of $1,800,000. The expected long-term rate of return is 7%. What is the expected return on plan assets used in computing pension expense?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $140,000 (based on fair value)

choices.label: B
choices.text: $126,000 (based on market-related value)

choices.label: C
choices.text: $133,000 (average of fair value and market-related value)

choices.label: D
choices.text: The actual return on plan assets

questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-30-35-22, the expected return on plan assets is calculated by multiplying the expected long-term rate of return by the market-related value of plan assets (which can be either fair value or a calculated value that smooths short-term market fluctuations over up to 5 years). Expected return = $1,800,000 × 7% = $126,000.
questions.explanationJa: 制度資産の期待収益は、長期期待収益率 × 市場関連価値で計算する。市場関連価値は公正価値、または最大5年間の市場変動を平準化した計算値のいずれかである。期待収益 = $1,800,000 × 7% = $126,000。

questions.stem: Which of the following must be disclosed for defined benefit pension plans?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The names of all plan participants

choices.label: B
choices.text: A reconciliation of the beginning and ending balances of both the PBO and the fair value of plan assets

choices.label: C
choices.text: Only the total pension expense for the year

choices.label: D
choices.text: Individual account balances for each employee

questions.explanation: ASC 715-20-50-1 requires extensive disclosures including: a reconciliation of PBO (showing service cost, interest cost, actuarial gains/losses, benefits paid, amendments), a reconciliation of plan assets (contributions, actual return, benefits paid), the components of net periodic pension cost, and the assumptions used (discount rate, expected return, salary growth rate).
questions.explanationJa: 確定給付制度の開示には、PBOの調整表（勤務費用・利息費用・数理損益・給付支払・制度改定）、制度資産の調整表（拠出金・実際収益・給付支払）、NPPCの構成要素、使用した前提条件（割引率・期待収益率・昇給率）が含まれる。

questions.stem: Apex Corp. has the following pension data for Year 2: Service cost $85,000; PBO at beginning of year $1,400,000; Discount rate 5%; Market-related value of plan assets at beginning of year $1,000,000; Expected return on assets 8%; Amortization of prior service cost $6,000; Amortization of net loss $7,000. What is Apex's net periodic pension cost for Year 2?
questions.explanation: NPPC = Service cost + Interest cost - Expected return + PSC amortization + Net loss amortization. Interest cost = $1,400,000 × 5% = $70,000. Expected return = $1,000,000 × 8% = $80,000. NPPC = $85,000 + $70,000 - $80,000 + $6,000 + $7,000 = $88,000.

questions.stem: Delta Corp.'s defined benefit plan has the following data: PBO at January 1 = $2,000,000; Service cost = $150,000; Discount rate = 5%; Benefits paid to retirees = $180,000; Actuarial loss from assumption change = $60,000; Plan amendment (prior service cost) = $80,000. What is the PBO at December 31?
questions.explanation: PBO rollforward: Beginning PBO + Service cost + Interest cost + Actuarial losses - Benefits paid + Plan amendments. Interest cost = $2,000,000 × 5% = $100,000. Ending PBO = $2,000,000 + $150,000 + $100,000 + $60,000 - $180,000 + $80,000 = $2,210,000.

questions.stem: Sigma Corp.'s pension plan has the following data for the year: Fair value of plan assets at January 1 = $900,000; Actual return on plan assets = $72,000; Employer contributions = $200,000; Benefits paid to retirees = $150,000; Administrative expenses paid from plan = $20,000. What is the fair value of plan assets at December 31?
questions.explanation: Plan assets rollforward: Beginning FV + Actual return + Employer contributions - Benefits paid - Administrative expenses. Ending FV = $900,000 + $72,000 + $200,000 - $150,000 - $20,000 = $1,002,000.
questions.explanationJa: 制度資産ロールフォワード：期首公正価値 + 実際収益 + 雇用主拠出金 - 給付支払 - 管理費用。期末公正価値 = $900,000 + $72,000 + $200,000 - $150,000 - $20,000 = $1,002,000。

questions.stem: At the beginning of Year 3, Omega Corp. has the following pension data: PBO = $2,400,000; Market-related value of plan assets = $2,100,000; Unrecognized net loss in AOCI = $290,000; Average remaining service period of active employees = 10 years. What is the minimum amortization of the net loss to be included in Year 3 pension cost?
questions.explanation: Corridor = 10% × greater of PBO ($2,400,000) or MRVA ($2,100,000) = 10% × $2,400,000 = $240,000. Net loss in AOCI ($290,000) exceeds the corridor ($240,000) by $50,000. Minimum amortization = excess ÷ average remaining service period = $50,000 ÷ 10 years = $5,000.

questions.stem: At December 31, Year 5, Gamma Corp. has a projected benefit obligation of $3,500,000 and plan assets with a fair value of $3,200,000. Additionally, the following amounts are in AOCI: unrecognized prior service cost $120,000 (debit) and unrecognized net loss $85,000 (debit). What amount should Gamma report as the net pension liability on its balance sheet?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-20-45-1, the balance sheet reports only the funded status: PBO minus fair value of plan assets. The AOCI items (prior service cost and net loss) are reported in stockholders' equity as AOCI components, not added to the pension liability. Funded status = $3,500,000 - $3,200,000 = $300,000 net liability.
questions.explanationJa: 貸借対照表には積立状況（funded status）のみを報告する。AOCI項目（過去勤務費用・純損失）は株主資本のAOCI構成要素であり、年金負債には加算しない。積立状況 = PBO $3,500,000 - 制度資産 $3,200,000 = $300,000の純負債。

questions.stem: Kappa Corp.'s pension plan has plan assets with a market-related value of $1,600,000 at the beginning of the year. The expected rate of return is 7%, and the actual return on plan assets for the year was $138,000. What amount of asset-related gain or loss is recognized in OCI for the year?
questions.explanation: Expected return = $1,600,000 × 7% = $112,000. Actual return = $138,000. The difference (actual minus expected) = $138,000 - $112,000 = $26,000 gain. For pension cost, only the expected return ($112,000) reduces NPPC. The unexpected excess of $26,000 is an asset-related gain recognized in OCI (credit to AOCI, reducing any accumulated net loss).
questions.explanationJa: 期待収益 = $1,600,000 × 7% = $112,000。実際収益 = $138,000。差額（実際 - 期待）= $138,000 - $112,000 = $26,000の利得。NPPCを減少させるのは期待収益（$112,000）のみであり、超過$26,000は資産関連利得としてOCIに認識する（AOCIの累積純損失を減少）。

questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Theta Corp. amends its pension plan, creating prior service cost of $180,000. The company has 60 active employees, each expected to work 3 more years (total remaining service years = 180). Using the years-of-service method, what is the amortization of prior service cost in Year 1?
questions.explanation: Years-of-service method: Total service years = 60 employees × 3 years = 180. Year 1 fraction: All 60 employees work in Year 1, so fraction = 60/180 = 1/3. Amortization = $180,000 × (60/180) = $60,000. Alternatively, if straight-line over average remaining service period (3 years): $180,000 / 3 = $60,000. Both methods yield the same result here because all employees have equal remaining service lives.

questions.stem: Lambda Corp. settles 25% of its pension obligation by purchasing annuity contracts. Before settlement, the PBO was $4,000,000, plan assets were $3,600,000 at fair value, and unrecognized net loss in AOCI was $200,000. The settlement cost (annuity purchase price) equals the proportionate share of the PBO settled ($1,000,000). What settlement loss should Lambda recognize?
questions.explanation: A settlement requires proportional recognition of unrecognized net gain/loss from AOCI. Lambda settles 25% of the PBO. The pro-rata share of unrecognized net loss recognized in earnings = 25% × $200,000 = $50,000. This is the settlement loss recognized in the income statement.
questions.explanationJa: 清算（セトルメント）ではAOCIの未認識純損益を按分して認識する。清算したPBOの割合は25%。認識する未認識純損失 = 25% × $200,000 = $50,000。これが損益計算書に計上される清算損失である。

questions.stem: Epsilon Corp. has a PBO of $2,500,000 at January 1. The discount rate is 6%. Service cost accrues evenly throughout the year at $200,000 total. Benefits of $180,000 are paid on June 30. What is the interest cost component of pension expense for the year (assuming interest accrues on beginning PBO for the full year, with no adjustment for mid-year cash flows per simplified approach)?
questions.explanation: Under the simplified approach commonly used in practice and CPA exam questions, interest cost is computed on the beginning-of-year PBO: Interest cost = $2,500,000 × 6% = $150,000. Mid-year service cost accruals and benefit payments do not adjust the interest calculation under this simplified method.
questions.explanationJa: 簡便法（CPA試験で一般的）では、利息費用を期首PBOに基づき計算する。利息費用 = $2,500,000 × 6% = $150,000。年中の勤務費用発生や給付支払は利息計算に影響しない。

questions.stem: Zeta Corp. sponsors a defined contribution (401k) plan. The plan requires the employer to match 100% of employee contributions up to 6% of salary. Total eligible employee salaries are $5,000,000. Employees contributed an average of 8% of their salaries. Additionally, the plan provides a discretionary profit-sharing contribution of 2% of salaries. What is Zeta's total pension expense for the defined contribution plan?
questions.explanation: Employer match: Employees contributed 8% but match is capped at 6% of salary. Match = $5,000,000 × 6% = $300,000. Profit-sharing contribution = $5,000,000 × 2% = $100,000. Total DC expense = $300,000 + $100,000 = $400,000. For defined contribution plans, the expense equals the required contribution for the period.
questions.explanationJa: 雇用主マッチング：従業員は8%拠出したが、マッチングは給与の6%が上限。マッチング = $5,000,000 × 6% = $300,000。利益分配拠出 = $5,000,000 × 2% = $100,000。DC費用合計 = $300,000 + $100,000 = $400,000。確定拠出制度では費用 = その期間の必要拠出額である。

questions.stem: Entity A has a defined benefit pension plan. The projected benefit obligation (PBO) is $5,000,000, which includes assumptions about future salary increases. If future salary increases are excluded, the obligation would be $4,200,000. What is the accumulated benefit obligation (ABO)?
questions.explanation: The accumulated benefit obligation (ABO) measures the obligation using current salary levels, without projecting future salary increases. The PBO includes future salary projections. Therefore, ABO = PBO minus the effect of projected salary increases = $4,200,000. The ABO is disclosed in the footnotes and is relevant for the minimum liability concept.
questions.explanationJa: 累積給付債務（ABO）は将来の昇給を見込まず、現在の給与水準に基づく債務を測定する。PBOは将来の昇給見込みを含む。したがって、ABO = PBO − 将来昇給見込みの影響 = $4,200,000。ABOは注記で開示され、最低負債の概念に関連する。

questions.stem: When ASC 715 was initially adopted, Entity B had an unrecognized net transition obligation of $600,000. Entity B elected to amortize it over the average remaining service period of active employees (20 years). What is the annual transition obligation amortization?

choices.label: D
choices.text: $0 because transition obligations are no longer relevant

questions.explanation: Under the original transition provisions of SFAS 87 (now ASC 715), entities could elect to amortize the transition obligation over the average remaining service period (minimum 15 years). Annual amortization = $600,000 / 20 years = $30,000. This amount is a component of net periodic pension cost. Most plans have fully amortized transition obligations by now.
questions.explanationJa: SFAS 87（現ASC 715）の当初移行規定により、企業は移行時差異を従業員の平均残存勤務期間（最低15年）にわたり償却する選択ができた。年間償却額 = $600,000 / 20年 = $30,000。この金額は正味期間年金費用の構成要素である。

questions.stem: Entity C amends its pension plan to increase benefits, resulting in prior service cost of $400,000. The average remaining service period of active employees is 10 years. In Year 1, how much prior service cost amortization is included in net periodic pension cost?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-30-35-10, prior service cost from a plan amendment is recognized in OCI at the amendment date, then amortized from AOCI into net periodic pension cost over the average remaining service period of active employees. Annual amortization = $400,000 / 10 years = $40,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-30-35-10により、制度改訂による過去勤務費用は改訂日にOCIで認識し、活動中従業員の平均残存勤務期間にわたりAOCIから正味期間年金費用へ償却する。年間償却額 = $400,000 / 10年 = $40,000。

questions.stem: Entity D participates in a multi-employer defined benefit pension plan. Under ASC 715-80, how should Entity D account for its participation?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Recognize its proportionate share of the plan's PBO and plan assets on its balance sheet

choices.label: B
choices.text: Recognize pension expense equal to the required contributions for the period

choices.label: C
choices.text: Apply single-employer pension accounting using estimated allocation of plan assets and PBO

choices.label: D
choices.text: Disclose the plan's existence but recognize no expense

questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-80-35-1, an employer participating in a multi-employer plan generally recognizes as pension expense the required contribution for the period. The employer does not recognize its share of the plan's assets or obligations. Additional disclosures are required, including the nature of the plan and any significant withdrawal liability.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-80-35-1により、複数事業主制度に参加する企業は、一般にその期間の必要拠出額を年金費用として認識する。制度の資産や債務の持分は認識しない。制度の性質や重要な脱退負債等の追加開示が必要である。

questions.stem: Entity E closes a division, significantly reducing the expected years of future service of its employees under the pension plan. The PBO is reduced by $300,000 due to the curtailment. Unrecognized prior service cost related to the curtailed employees is $120,000. What curtailment gain or loss should Entity E recognize?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Net curtailment gain of $180,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Net curtailment loss of $120,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Net curtailment gain of $300,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Net curtailment loss of $180,000

questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-30-35-92, a curtailment includes accelerated recognition of prior service cost. The unrecognized prior service cost ($120,000) is recognized as a loss. The reduction in PBO ($300,000) is recognized as a gain. Net curtailment effect = $300,000 gain − $120,000 loss = $180,000 net gain.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-30-35-92により、制度縮小では過去勤務費用の加速認識を含む。未認識過去勤務費用（$120,000）は損失として認識。PBOの減少（$300,000）は利得として認識。正味縮小効果 = $300,000利得 − $120,000損失 = $180,000の正味利得。

questions.stem: Entity F offers special termination benefits to employees who accept early retirement within 60 days. The estimated cost is $500,000 and 80% of eligible employees are expected to accept. When should Entity F recognize the cost?

choices.label: A
choices.text: When the offer is communicated to employees and acceptance is probable

choices.label: B
choices.text: When employees formally accept the offer

choices.label: C
choices.text: When the benefit payments begin

choices.label: D
choices.text: Ratably over the remaining service period of accepting employees

questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-30-25-4, special (one-time) termination benefits are recognized as a liability and expense when the employees accept the offer and the amount can be reasonably estimated. This differs from contractual termination benefits, which are recognized when it is probable that employees will be entitled to benefits.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-30-25-4により、特別（一時的）終了給付は従業員がオファーを受諾し金額が合理的に見積もれる時点で負債と費用を認識する。これは、従業員が給付を受ける可能性が高い時点で認識する契約上の終了給付とは異なる。

questions.stem: Which of the following events would require an interim remeasurement of a defined benefit pension plan's net benefit obligation and plan assets?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A significant change in market interest rates

choices.label: B
choices.text: A plan amendment, curtailment, or settlement

choices.label: C
choices.text: A change in the company's stock price

choices.label: D
choices.text: A change in the number of plan participants due to normal turnover

questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-30-35-63, remeasurement of plan assets and obligations is required when a significant event occurs, such as a plan amendment, curtailment, or settlement. Routine changes in market conditions, stock prices, or normal employee turnover do not trigger interim remeasurement.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-30-35-63により、制度改訂、縮小、清算等の重要な事象が発生した場合、制度資産と債務の再測定が必要となる。市場状況の通常の変動、株価の変動、通常の従業員異動では中間期間の再測定は行わない。

questions.stem: Under ASC 715, which of the following actuarial assumptions must be disclosed in the financial statements for a defined benefit pension plan?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Discount rate, expected return on plan assets, and rate of compensation increase

choices.label: B
choices.text: Only the discount rate

choices.label: C
choices.text: Discount rate and expected return on plan assets only

choices.label: D
choices.text: No specific assumptions need to be disclosed

questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-20-50-1, entities must disclose the weighted-average assumptions used to determine benefit obligations (discount rate, rate of compensation increase) and net periodic benefit cost (discount rate, expected long-term return on plan assets, rate of compensation increase). All three are key required disclosures.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-20-50-1により、給付債務の算定に使用した加重平均仮定（割引率、昇給率）と正味期間給付費用の算定に使用した仮定（割引率、制度資産の期待長期収益率、昇給率）を開示しなければならない。3つとも主要な必須開示事項である。

questions.stem: Entity G's pension plan has a projected benefit obligation (PBO) of $10,000,000 and an accumulated benefit obligation (ABO) of $8,500,000. Of the ABO, $7,200,000 relates to employees who have met the vesting requirements. What is the vested benefit obligation (VBO)?
questions.explanation: The vested benefit obligation (VBO) is the portion of the ABO that employees are entitled to receive regardless of their future employment status. It includes only the benefits of employees who have met the vesting criteria. VBO = $7,200,000. The relationship is: VBO ≤ ABO ≤ PBO.
questions.explanationJa: 既得給付債務（VBO）は、従業員が将来の雇用状況にかかわらず受け取る権利を有するABOの部分。受給権確定要件を満たした従業員の給付のみを含む。VBO = $7,200,000。関係式: VBO ≤ ABO ≤ PBO。

questions.subtopic: OPEB - Health Care Cost Trend Rate
questions.stem: Entity H sponsors a postretirement health care plan (OPEB). The current health care cost trend rate is 8%, decreasing by 1% per year to an ultimate rate of 5%. The current year's expected claims cost is $1,000,000. What is the expected claims cost in Year 2?
questions.explanation: The health care cost trend rate for Year 1 is 8%. Expected claims cost in Year 2 = $1,000,000 × (1 + 8%) = $1,080,000. The trend rate decreases by 1% per year (Year 2 rate would be 7% for projecting Year 3 costs, etc.) until reaching the ultimate rate of 5%.
questions.explanationJa: Year 1の医療費トレンド率は8%。Year 2の予想給付額 = $1,000,000 × (1 + 8%) = $1,080,000。トレンド率は年1%ずつ低下し（Year 2のレートは7%でYear 3コストの予測に使用等）、最終率5%に達するまで続く。

questions.stem: Entity J has two defined benefit pension plans. Plan A has a PBO of $3,000,000 and plan assets of $3,500,000 (overfunded by $500,000). Plan B has a PBO of $2,000,000 and plan assets of $1,600,000 (underfunded by $400,000). How should Entity J report these on the balance sheet?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Net asset of $100,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Noncurrent asset of $500,000 and noncurrent liability of $400,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Noncurrent asset of $100,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Noncurrent liability of $400,000 only

questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-30-45-1, the funded status of each plan must be recognized separately on the balance sheet. Overfunded plans are reported as assets and underfunded plans as liabilities. Netting across plans is not permitted. Plan A: asset of $500,000. Plan B: liability of $400,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-30-45-1により、各制度の積立状況を貸借対照表に個別に認識する。過剰積立の制度は資産、積立不足の制度は負債として報告する。制度間の相殺は認められない。Plan A: 資産$500,000。Plan B: 負債$400,000。

questions.subtopic: Net Periodic Pension Cost - Full Calculation
questions.stem: Entity K provides the following pension data: Service cost $200,000; Interest cost $150,000; Expected return on plan assets $180,000; Amortization of prior service cost $25,000; Amortization of net actuarial loss $15,000. What is the net periodic pension cost?
questions.explanation: Net periodic pension cost = Service cost + Interest cost − Expected return on plan assets + Amortization of prior service cost + Amortization of net loss = $200,000 + $150,000 − $180,000 + $25,000 + $15,000 = $210,000. Under ASU 2017-07, only service cost is presented in operating income; other components are below operating income.
questions.explanationJa: 正味期間年金費用 = 勤務費用 + 利息費用 − 制度資産の期待収益 + 過去勤務費用の償却 + 正味数理計算上の損失の償却 = $200,000 + $150,000 − $180,000 + $25,000 + $15,000 = $210,000。ASU 2017-07により、勤務費用のみ営業利益に表示し、他の構成要素は営業利益の下に表示する。

questions.stem: Entity L's pension plan has plan assets of $6,000,000 and a PBO of $5,000,000. However, the plan has a provision that limits the amount Entity L can withdraw from the plan. Under IFRS (IAS 19), but NOT under US GAAP, what concept limits the recognition of the net pension asset?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The asset ceiling test, which limits the asset to the present value of economic benefits available

choices.label: B
choices.text: The corridor approach

choices.label: C
choices.text: The minimum liability requirement

choices.label: D
choices.text: There is no limit under either IFRS or US GAAP

questions.explanation: Under IAS 19, the net defined benefit asset is limited to the asset ceiling — the present value of economic benefits available in the form of refunds or reductions in future contributions. US GAAP (ASC 715) does not have an equivalent asset ceiling test. The entire funded status (overfunded amount) is recognized under US GAAP.
questions.explanationJa: IAS 19では、確定給付資産の純額は資産上限（将来の拠出金の返還または減額の形で利用可能な経済的便益の現在価値）に制限される。US GAAP（ASC 715）には同等の資産上限テストはない。US GAAPでは積立状況（過剰積立額）全体が認識される。

questions.stem: Entity M eliminates the accrual of defined benefits for a significant number of active participants. Before the curtailment, the unrecognized net actuarial loss is $500,000 and unrecognized prior service cost is $200,000. The PBO decreases by $800,000. What is the curtailment gain?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-30-35-92, curtailment accounting requires: (1) recognize the unrecognized prior service cost ($200,000 loss) for the affected employees, (2) recognize a proportionate share of the unrecognized net loss, and (3) recognize the gain from PBO reduction ($800,000). Net curtailment = $800,000 − $200,000 (PSC) − $500,000 (net loss) = $100,000 gain.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-30-35-92の制度縮小会計：(1) 影響を受ける従業員の未認識過去勤務費用を認識（$200,000の損失）、(2) 未認識正味損失の比例配分額を認識（$500,000）、(3) PBO減少からの利得を認識（$800,000）。正味縮小効果 = $800,000 − $200,000 − $500,000 = $100,000の利得。

questions.stem: Entity N's pension plan has an accumulated net actuarial loss of $800,000 in AOCI. The greater of the PBO ($4,000,000) or the market-related value of plan assets ($3,500,000) is $4,000,000. The corridor is 10% × $4,000,000 = $400,000. The average remaining service period is 10 years. What amount of actuarial loss is amortized from AOCI in the current year?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-30-35-24, the excess of accumulated net actuarial loss over the corridor is amortized. Excess = $800,000 − $400,000 = $400,000. Minimum amortization = $400,000 / 10 years = $40,000. This corridor approach provides a minimum; entities may elect a faster amortization method.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-30-35-24により、累積正味数理計算上の損失がコリドーを超える部分を償却する。超過額 = $800,000 − $400,000 = $400,000。最低償却額 = $400,000 / 10年 = $40,000。このコリドー方式は最低額を規定し、より速い償却方法を選択することも可能。

questions.stem: Entity P merges two of its defined benefit pension plans into a single plan. Under ASC 715, how should the combined plan be accounted for after the merger?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The combined plan's PBO and plan assets are measured as the sum of both plans' amounts

choices.label: B
choices.text: The plans must be remeasured at fair value as of the merger date, and any gain/loss is recognized in income

choices.label: C
choices.text: The smaller plan is treated as a curtailment before merger

choices.label: D
choices.text: The combined plan is treated as a new plan, and all prior accumulated OCI is recognized in income

questions.explanation: When an entity merges two of its own plans, the PBO and plan assets of both plans are combined. The AOCI balances (prior service cost, net actuarial gains/losses) are also combined. No gain or loss is recognized solely from the plan merger. The combined plan is measured going forward as a single plan.
questions.explanationJa: 企業が自身の2つの制度を統合する場合、両制度のPBOと制度資産を合算する。AOCI残高（過去勤務費用、正味数理計算上の損益）も合算する。制度統合のみでは損益を認識しない。統合後の制度は単一の制度として将来に向けて測定する。

questions.stem: Entity Q's pension plan experienced an actual return on plan assets of $300,000 versus an expected return of $250,000 in the current year. How is the $50,000 difference initially recognized?

choices.label: A
choices.text: In net periodic pension cost as an actuarial gain

choices.label: B
choices.text: In other comprehensive income (OCI)

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a direct increase to retained earnings

choices.label: D
choices.text: Not recognized until the plan is settled

questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-30-35-26, the difference between actual and expected return on plan assets is an asset gain that is initially recognized in OCI. Only the expected return ($250,000) is used to compute net periodic pension cost. The $50,000 gain accumulates in AOCI and is subject to future amortization through the corridor approach.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-30-35-26により、制度資産の実際収益と期待収益の差額は資産利得としてOCIで当初認識する。正味期間年金費用の計算には期待収益（$250,000）のみを使用する。$50,000の利得はAOCIに蓄積され、コリドー方式による将来の償却の対象となる。

questions.stem: Entity R has a written OPEB plan that provides retiree health benefits at 80% employer-paid. However, for the past 5 years, Entity R has actually paid 100% of retiree health costs. Under ASC 715-60, which plan should be used to measure the APBO?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The written plan (80% employer-paid)

choices.label: B
choices.text: The substantive plan (100% employer-paid based on past practice)

choices.label: C
choices.text: The average of the written and substantive plans (90%)

choices.label: D
choices.text: Whichever plan results in the lower obligation

questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-60-35-5, if the substantive plan differs from the written plan, the substantive plan is the basis for accounting. The substantive plan is communicated through past practice, stated intent, or company policy. Since Entity R consistently paid 100% for 5 years, the substantive plan (100%) should be used to measure the accumulated postretirement benefit obligation (APBO).
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-60-35-5により、実質的な制度が文書上の制度と異なる場合、実質的な制度を会計処理の基礎とする。実質的な制度は過去の慣行、表明された意図、会社方針で伝達される。Entity Rは5年間一貫して100%を支払っているため、実質的な制度（100%）をAPBOの測定に使用する。

questions.stem: Entity S amends its postretirement health benefit plan (OPEB) to reduce future benefits for active employees. This results in negative prior service cost (a credit) of $600,000. How should this be accounted for?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Recognize the $600,000 credit immediately in income

choices.label: B
choices.text: Recognize the credit in OCI, then amortize from AOCI to reduce net periodic postretirement benefit cost over the average remaining service period

choices.label: C
choices.text: Reduce the APBO and recognize a settlement gain

choices.label: D
choices.text: Record the credit against goodwill

questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-60-35-15, negative prior service cost (a plan amendment that reduces benefits) is recognized in OCI and then amortized from AOCI as a credit (reduction) to net periodic postretirement benefit cost over the average remaining service period of active employees. It is not recognized immediately in income.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-60-35-15により、負の過去勤務費用（給付を減額する制度改訂）はOCIで認識し、活動中従業員の平均残存勤務期間にわたりAOCIから正味期間退職後給付費用の貸方（減額）として償却する。即時に利益で認識しない。

questions.stem: Entity T's defined benefit pension plan has a PBO of $10,000,000 measured using a 5% discount rate. The actuary estimates that a 1% decrease in the discount rate would increase the PBO by $1,200,000. What would the PBO be if the discount rate decreased to 4%?
questions.explanation: The sensitivity analysis shows that a 1% decrease in the discount rate (from 5% to 4%) increases the PBO by $1,200,000. New PBO = $10,000,000 + $1,200,000 = $11,200,000. Discount rate sensitivity is a required disclosure under ASC 715-20-50-1 to help users understand the effect of assumptions on the obligation.
questions.explanationJa: 感応度分析により、割引率の1%低下（5%から4%）でPBOが$1,200,000増加する。新PBO = $10,000,000 + $1,200,000 = $11,200,000。割引率の感応度はASC 715-20-50-1の必須開示事項であり、仮定が債務に与える影響の理解を助ける。

questions.subtopic: Components of Net Periodic Pension Cost
questions.stem: Which of the following is NOT a component of net periodic pension cost under ASC 715?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Service cost

choices.label: B
choices.text: Interest cost

choices.label: C
choices.text: Contributions to the plan

choices.label: D
choices.text: Amortization of prior service cost

questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-30-35-4, the components of net periodic pension cost are: (1) service cost, (2) interest cost, (3) expected return on plan assets, (4) amortization of prior service cost, (5) amortization of net actuarial gain or loss, and (6) amortization of transition obligation/asset. Employer contributions are cash flows TO the plan that increase plan assets but are NOT a component of pension expense.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-30-35-4により、正味年金費用の構成要素は：(1)勤務費用、(2)利息費用、(3)期待運用収益、(4)過去勤務費用の償却、(5)数理差異の償却、(6)移行時債務の償却。掛金拠出は制度資産を増加させるキャッシュフローであり、年金費用の構成要素ではない。

questions.stem: Under ASU 2017-07, where should the components of net periodic pension cost be presented in the income statement?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Service cost in operating income; all other components outside of operating income (or in a separately disclosed line)

choices.label: B
choices.text: All components in operating income

choices.label: C
choices.text: All components below operating income

choices.label: D
choices.text: Service cost in COGS; other components in SG&A

questions.explanation: Under ASU 2017-07 (ASC 715-30-45-3A), only the service cost component of net periodic pension cost is presented in the same line item as other employee compensation costs (typically within operating income). All other components (interest cost, expected return, amortizations, etc.) are presented outside of operating income or in a separately identified line item, unless they are eligible for capitalization.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2017-07（ASC 715-30-45-3A）により、勤務費用のみが他の従業員報酬と同じ項目（通常は営業利益内）に表示される。その他の構成要素（利息費用、期待収益、償却等）は営業利益外に表示する。

questions.subtopic: Actual vs Expected Return - Asset Gain/Loss
questions.stem: Plan assets have a beginning balance of $8,000,000. The expected return is 7%, and the actual return for the year is 10%. What is the asset gain or loss recognized in OCI?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $240,000 gain in OCI

choices.label: B
choices.text: $240,000 loss in OCI

choices.label: C
choices.text: $800,000 gain in OCI

choices.label: D
choices.text: $560,000 gain in OCI

questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-30-35-22, the difference between actual return and expected return on plan assets is the asset gain or loss. Expected return = $8,000,000 × 7% = $560,000. Actual return = $8,000,000 × 10% = $800,000. Asset gain = $800,000 − $560,000 = $240,000. This gain is recognized in OCI (not in pension expense), and is subsequently subject to amortization from AOCI into pension expense using the corridor approach.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-30-35-22により、実際収益と期待収益の差額が資産損益。期待収益 = $560,000。実際収益 = $800,000。資産利得 = $240,000。OCIに認識し、その後コリドー法を通じてAOCIから年金費用に償却される。

questions.stem: At the beginning of Year 3, Entity A has: PBO $12,000,000, Plan Assets $10,000,000, Net actuarial loss in AOCI $2,500,000. Average remaining service period of active employees is 10 years. What is the minimum amortization of net actuarial loss in Year 3?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-30-35-24, the corridor equals 10% of the greater of the PBO or plan assets at the beginning of the year. Corridor = 10% × max($12,000,000, $10,000,000) = $1,200,000. Net actuarial loss ($2,500,000) exceeds the corridor by $2,500,000 − $1,200,000 = $1,300,000. Minimum amortization = $1,300,000 / 10 years = $130,000.

questions.stem: Entity B amends its pension plan on January 1, Year 1, granting retroactive benefits that increase the PBO by $600,000. The average remaining service period of affected employees is 12 years. What is the amortization of prior service cost in Year 1?

choices.label: C
choices.text: $0 — prior service cost is not amortized

questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-30-35-12, prior service cost from a plan amendment is recognized in OCI at the amendment date and then amortized into net periodic pension cost over the average remaining service period of affected employees. Annual amortization = $600,000 / 12 years = $50,000. Alternatively, entities may use a years-of-service method if more representative.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-30-35-12により、制度変更による過去勤務費用は変更日にOCIに認識し、影響を受ける従業員の平均残存勤務期間で年金費用に償却。年間償却 = $600,000 ÷ 12年 = $50,000。

questions.stem: Entity C settles its pension obligation for a group of retirees by purchasing annuity contracts. The PBO for the settled group is $3,000,000 (out of total PBO of $15,000,000). The settlement cost (annuity purchase price) is $3,200,000. The unrecognized net actuarial loss in AOCI is $1,500,000. What gain or loss is recognized from the settlement?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Recognize proportionate AOCI loss of $300,000 plus settlement loss of $200,000 = $500,000 total loss

choices.label: B
choices.text: $200,000 loss only

choices.label: C
choices.text: $300,000 loss only

choices.label: D
choices.text: $0 because settlements are recognized in OCI

questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-30-35-68, settlement accounting requires recognition of a proportionate share of the unrecognized net gain or loss from AOCI. Proportion settled = $3,000,000 / $15,000,000 = 20%. AOCI reclassified = $1,500,000 × 20% = $300,000. Additionally, if the settlement cost ($3,200,000) exceeds the PBO settled ($3,000,000), a settlement loss of $200,000 is recognized. Total loss = $300,000 + $200,000 = $500,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-30-35-68により、決済会計ではAOCIの未認識損益の比例部分を認識。決済比率 = 20%。AOCI振替 = $1,500,000 × 20% = $300,000。決済費用がPBOを超過：$200,000。合計損失 = $500,000。

questions.stem: Which of the following is a key difference between pension (ASC 715-30) and other postretirement benefit (OPEB) (ASC 715-60) accounting?

choices.label: A
choices.text: OPEB plans are typically unfunded and use an Accumulated Postretirement Benefit Obligation (APBO) instead of PBO

choices.label: B
choices.text: OPEB plans do not recognize service cost

choices.label: C
choices.text: OPEB plans are always funded through trusts

choices.label: D
choices.text: OPEB does not require balance sheet recognition of funded status

questions.explanation: A key difference is that OPEB plans (such as retiree health insurance) are typically unfunded or significantly underfunded. The obligation is measured as the Accumulated Postretirement Benefit Obligation (APBO), which is conceptually similar to PBO but incorporates healthcare cost trends. Both pension and OPEB plans require funded status recognition on the balance sheet (ASC 715-60-25), both recognize service cost, but OPEB often has more complex trend rate assumptions.
questions.explanationJa: 主な違いは、OPEB制度（退職者医療保険等）は通常未積立または大幅に積立不足であること。債務はAPBO（累積退職後給付債務）で測定され、医療費トレンドを反映する点でPBOと異なる。両方とも積立状態のBS認識が必要。

questions.stem: Entity D's PBO at the beginning of the year is $20,000,000. During the year: service cost $800,000, interest cost $1,000,000, benefits paid $1,500,000, actuarial loss $400,000, and plan amendment increasing PBO by $300,000. What is the ending PBO?
questions.explanation: PBO rollforward: Beginning PBO $20,000,000 + Service cost $800,000 + Interest cost $1,000,000 − Benefits paid ($1,500,000) + Actuarial loss $400,000 + Prior service cost (plan amendment) $300,000 = Ending PBO $21,000,000. Benefits paid reduce the PBO because the obligation to those retirees has been settled.

questions.stem: Which of the following is required regarding the measurement of pension plan assets under ASC 715?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Plan assets are measured at fair value

choices.label: B
choices.text: Plan assets are measured at amortized cost

choices.label: C
choices.text: Plan assets are measured at the lower of cost or market

choices.label: D
choices.text: Plan assets are measured at historical cost plus accumulated earnings

questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-30-35-60, plan assets used to fund a defined benefit pension obligation are measured at fair value at the measurement date. Fair value is determined in accordance with ASC 820. This measurement is used both for the funded status calculation on the balance sheet and for determining the expected return on plan assets component of pension expense.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-30-35-60により、確定給付年金制度の制度資産は測定日の公正価値で測定する。公正価値はASC 820に従い決定。この測定はBS上の積立状態の計算と年金費用の期待収益率の計算の両方に使用される。

questions.stem: Entity E closes a division, eliminating the future service of 200 employees under its defined benefit plan. The PBO reduction from the curtailment is $1,500,000. The unrecognized prior service cost attributable to the eliminated employees is $400,000. What is the curtailment gain or loss?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Curtailment gain of $1,100,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Curtailment gain of $1,500,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Curtailment loss of $400,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Curtailment gain of $1,500,000 and recognition of $400,000 prior service cost

questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-30-35-92, a curtailment results in: (1) recognition of the unrecognized prior service cost associated with years of service no longer expected ($400,000 loss recognized from AOCI), and (2) a gain or loss from the change in PBO ($1,500,000 gain from PBO reduction). Net curtailment gain = $1,500,000 − $400,000 = $1,100,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-30-35-92により、縮小会計では：(1)もはや期待されない勤務年数に関連する未認識過去勤務費用を認識（$400,000損失）、(2)PBO変動による損益（$1,500,000利得）。純縮小利得 = $1,500,000 − $400,000 = $1,100,000。

questions.stem: Entity F has a defined contribution (401(k)) plan where it matches 50% of employee contributions up to 6% of salary. An employee with a $100,000 salary contributes 8% ($8,000). What is Entity F's matching expense?
questions.explanation: The employer matches 50% of employee contributions up to 6% of salary. The employee contributes 8%, but the match only applies to the first 6% = $6,000. Employer match = 50% × $6,000 = $3,000. The additional 2% ($2,000) contributed by the employee above the 6% threshold receives no match. For defined contribution plans, the employer's expense equals its required contribution for the period.
questions.explanationJa: 雇用主は従業員拠出の給与6%分までの50%をマッチング。6%分 = $6,000。マッチング = 50% × $6,000 = $3,000。6%超の拠出にはマッチングなし。確定拠出制度では、雇用主の費用 = 当期の必要拠出額。

questions.stem: Under ASC 715, what date must be used to measure the plan assets and benefit obligations of a defined benefit pension plan?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The date of the employer's fiscal year-end statement of financial position

choices.label: B
choices.text: Any date within three months of the fiscal year-end

choices.label: C
choices.text: The anniversary date of the plan's inception

choices.label: D
choices.text: December 31 of each year, regardless of fiscal year-end

questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-30-35-63, the measurement date for plan assets and benefit obligations is the date of the employer's statement of financial position (the fiscal year-end date). Prior to ASC 715's update (ASU 2006-12), entities could use earlier measurement dates, but this is no longer permitted. The measurement date aligns with the balance sheet date to ensure the funded status reflects current conditions.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-30-35-63により、制度資産と給付債務の測定日は雇用主の財政状態報告書の日付（事業年度末）。以前は早い測定日も認められていたが、現在は不可。測定日はBS日と一致し、積立状態が現在の状況を反映。

questions.subtopic: Expected Return vs Actual Return - Pension Expense Effect
questions.stem: Entity G's pension plan has beginning plan assets of $5,000,000. The expected long-term rate of return is 8%. During the year, the actual return on plan assets was $600,000. What amount is used in calculating net periodic pension cost?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $400,000 expected return (reduces pension cost)

choices.label: B
choices.text: $600,000 actual return (reduces pension cost)

choices.label: C
choices.text: $200,000 difference (increases pension cost)

choices.label: D
choices.text: $500,000 (average of expected and actual)

questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-30-35-22, the expected return on plan assets (not the actual return) is used in calculating net periodic pension cost. Expected return = $5,000,000 × 8% = $400,000, which reduces pension expense. The difference between actual return ($600,000) and expected return ($400,000) = $200,000 asset gain, which is recognized in OCI and subject to the corridor amortization approach.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-30-35-22により、正味年金費用の計算には期待収益（実際収益ではなく）を使用する。期待収益 = $5,000,000 × 8% = $400,000（年金費用を減額）。実際収益$600,000との差額$200,000はOCIに認識。

questions.stem: Entity H participates in a multi-employer pension plan. Entity H decides to withdraw from the plan. Under ERISA, what financial obligation may Entity H face?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Withdrawal liability equal to Entity H's proportionate share of the plan's unfunded vested benefits

choices.label: B
choices.text: No liability because multi-employer plans are the union's responsibility

choices.label: C
choices.text: A liability limited to the contributions paid in the last 3 years

choices.label: D
choices.text: Only future contribution obligations

questions.explanation: Under ERISA and ASC 715-80-35-3, when an employer withdraws from a multi-employer plan, it may be assessed a withdrawal liability equal to its proportionate share of the plan's unfunded vested benefits. This can be a significant amount and must be recognized as a liability when the withdrawal occurs or when it becomes probable. The withdrawal liability is calculated based on contribution history and the plan's funded status.
questions.explanationJa: ERISAおよびASC 715-80-35-3により、複数事業主制度から脱退する場合、制度の未積立確定給付の比例持分に相当する脱退負債が課される可能性がある。脱退時または確実となった時点で負債を認識。拠出履歴と積立状態に基づき算定。

questions.stem: Entity J amends its pension plan to reduce future benefits, decreasing the PBO by $800,000. The unrecognized prior service cost in AOCI from a previous plan amendment is $200,000 (debit balance). How is the negative plan amendment accounted for?

choices.label: A
choices.text: First reduce the AOCI prior service cost debit by $200,000, then recognize the remaining $600,000 as a credit to AOCI (negative prior service cost)

choices.label: B
choices.text: Recognize $800,000 gain immediately in income

choices.label: C
choices.text: No accounting entry; negative amendments are not recognized

choices.label: D
choices.text: Record $800,000 as a credit to Retained Earnings

questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-30-35-11, a negative plan amendment (reducing benefits) first reduces any existing prior service cost (debit) in AOCI from previous amendments. The $200,000 existing debit is eliminated. The remaining $600,000 ($800,000 − $200,000) is recognized as negative prior service cost (credit) in AOCI. This credit is amortized into pension cost as a reduction to pension expense over the remaining service period.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-30-35-11により、給付減額の制度変更（負の制度変更）は、まずAOCIの既存の過去勤務費用（借方$200,000）を減額。残り$600,000は負の過去勤務費用（AOCI貸方）として認識し、残存勤務期間で年金費用の減額として償却。

questions.stem: Entity K offers enhanced retirement benefits to employees who voluntarily retire within the next 60 days. The additional cost is estimated at $2,000,000. When should Entity K recognize the cost of these special termination benefits?

choices.label: A
choices.text: When the offer is accepted by the employees and the amount can be reasonably estimated

choices.label: B
choices.text: Over the remaining expected service period of the employees

choices.label: C
choices.text: When the employees actually retire

choices.label: D
choices.text: Ratably over the 60-day offer period

questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-30-25-8, special termination benefits (those offered for a short period to encourage voluntary termination) are recognized as a liability and a loss when the employees accept the offer and the amount can be reasonably estimated. Unlike regular termination benefits, special termination benefits are event-driven and recognized upon acceptance, not over a service period.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-30-25-8により、特別解雇給付（自発的退職を促すための短期間のオファー）は、従業員がオファーを受諾し金額が合理的に見積可能な時点で負債と損失を認識する。通常の解雇給付と異なり、事象発生型で受諾時に認識。

questions.subtopic: OPEB - Health Care Cost Trend Rate
questions.stem: Entity L's OPEB plan provides retiree health benefits. The initial health care cost trend rate is 7%, declining 0.5% per year to an ultimate rate of 4.5%. In Year 1, the per capita health care cost is $10,000. What is the estimated per capita cost in Year 2?
questions.explanation: The health care cost trend rate in Year 1 is 7% (the initial rate). Year 2 cost = Year 1 cost × (1 + trend rate) = $10,000 × 1.07 = $10,700. In Year 2, the trend rate would decline to 6.5% for projecting Year 3 costs. The trend rate eventually reaches the ultimate rate of 4.5% and remains there. The assumed health care cost trend rate is a critical assumption in measuring OPEB obligations.
questions.explanationJa: Year 1の医療費トレンド率は7%（初期レート）。Year 2コスト = $10,000 × 1.07 = $10,700。Year 2ではトレンド率は6.5%に低下（Year 3以降に適用）。最終的に4.5%に達し一定となる。医療費トレンド率はOPEB債務測定の重要な仮定。

questions.stem: Entity M has a defined benefit plan with PBO of $15,000,000 and fair value of plan assets of $17,000,000. How is the funded status reported on the balance sheet?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a noncurrent asset of $2,000,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a current asset of $2,000,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Netted against other pension liabilities

choices.label: D
choices.text: Disclosed in the notes but not on the balance sheet

questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-30-25-1, the funded status of a defined benefit plan (fair value of plan assets minus PBO) is recognized on the balance sheet. When plan assets exceed PBO, the plan is overfunded, and the excess ($17,000,000 − $15,000,000 = $2,000,000) is reported as a noncurrent asset (prepaid pension benefit). An overfunded plan is presented separately from any underfunded plans — netting is not permitted across separate plans.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-30-25-1により、確定給付制度の積立状態（制度資産 − PBO）をBSに認識。制度資産がPBOを超過する場合、超過額$2,000,000を非流動資産（前払年金給付）として報告。過剰積立と不足積立の制度間の相殺は認められない。

questions.stem: Under ASC 715, which of the following correctly lists all components of net periodic pension cost?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Service cost, interest cost, expected return on plan assets, amortization of prior service cost, and gain or loss amortization

choices.label: B
choices.text: Service cost, interest cost, actual return on plan assets, and amortization of prior service cost

choices.label: C
choices.text: Service cost, interest cost, employer contributions, and amortization of net gain or loss

choices.label: D
choices.text: Service cost, interest cost, expected return on plan assets, and benefit payments to retirees

questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-30-35-4, net periodic pension cost consists of the following components: (1) service cost, (2) interest cost, (3) expected return on plan assets (which reduces pension expense), (4) amortization of prior service cost, (5) amortization of net gain or loss (from the corridor), and (6) amortization of any transition obligation. The expected return (not actual return) is used. Employer contributions and benefit payments are cash flows, not expense components. Answer is A.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-30-35-4により、正味期間年金費用は(1)勤務費用、(2)利息費用、(3)制度資産の期待収益（費用を減少）、(4)過去勤務費用の償却、(5)純損益の償却（回廊方式）、(6)移行時債務の償却で構成される。実際収益ではなく期待収益を使用する。雇用主拠出金や給付支払はキャッシュフローであり費用構成要素ではない。

questions.subtopic: Actuarial Assumption Changes - Effect on PBO
questions.stem: A company changes its mortality assumption to reflect longer life expectancies for plan participants. What is the most likely effect on the projected benefit obligation (PBO)?

choices.label: A
choices.text: PBO decreases because fewer benefits will be paid

choices.label: B
choices.text: PBO increases because benefits will be paid over a longer period

choices.label: C
choices.text: PBO is unaffected because mortality is not an actuarial assumption under ASC 715

choices.label: D
choices.text: PBO decreases because the discount rate is adjusted upward

questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-30-35-14, changes in actuarial assumptions (including mortality, turnover, and retirement age) result in actuarial gains or losses. If participants are expected to live longer, the plan must pay benefits for a longer period, increasing the present value of the total expected benefit payments. This increases the PBO. The change is recognized as an actuarial loss in OCI. Answer is B.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-30-35-14により、死亡率・離職率・退職年齢などの数理計算上の仮定変更は数理計算上の損益をもたらす。参加者がより長生きする見込みとなれば、給付支払期間が長くなり、予想給付支払総額の現在価値が増加するため、PBOは増加する。この変更はOCIに数理計算上の損失として認識される。

questions.stem: Under ASC 715, which rate should be used as the discount rate to calculate the projected benefit obligation (PBO)?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The expected long-term rate of return on plan assets

choices.label: B
choices.text: The rate at which the pension benefits could be effectively settled

choices.label: C
choices.text: The prime rate as published by the Federal Reserve

choices.label: D
choices.text: The company's weighted average cost of capital (WACC)

questions.explanation: ASC 715-30-35-43 states that the discount rate should reflect the rate at which the pension benefits could be effectively settled. In practice, this is based on current rates of return on high-quality fixed-income investments (such as AA-rated corporate bonds) whose cash flows match the timing and amount of expected benefit payments. The expected return on plan assets is a different assumption used for pension expense, not discounting. Answer is B.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-30-35-43により、割引率は年金給付を実質的に決済できる利率を反映すべきとされる。実務上、予想給付支払の時期と金額に合致する高品質確定利付投資（AA格社債等）の現行利回りに基づく。制度資産の期待収益率は年金費用計算に用いる別の仮定であり、割引には使用しない。

questions.stem: During 2025, a company's defined benefit plan had the following: fair value of plan assets at January 1 of $800,000, expected return on plan assets of $56,000, and actual return on plan assets of $72,000. What amount is recognized in other comprehensive income (OCI) related to the asset return?
questions.explanation: The difference between actual return and expected return on plan assets is an asset gain or loss recognized in OCI. Actual return = $72,000, Expected return = $56,000. The difference is $72,000 − $56,000 = $16,000. Since actual exceeded expected, this is a gain in OCI. Only the expected return ($56,000) reduces net periodic pension cost; the $16,000 excess goes to OCI. Answer is A.
questions.explanationJa: 制度資産の実際収益と期待収益の差額は、OCIに認識される資産損益である。実際収益$72,000 − 期待収益$56,000 = $16,000。実際が期待を上回ったため、OCIに$16,000の利得を認識する。正味期間年金費用を減少させるのは期待収益（$56,000）のみで、超過分$16,000はOCIに計上される。

questions.subtopic: Benefit Payments Effect on PBO and Plan Assets
questions.stem: During 2025, a company's pension plan paid $500,000 in benefits to retired employees. What is the effect of these benefit payments on the projected benefit obligation (PBO) and plan assets?

choices.label: A
choices.text: PBO decreases by $500,000; plan assets decrease by $500,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: PBO decreases by $500,000; plan assets are unchanged

choices.label: C
choices.text: PBO is unchanged; plan assets decrease by $500,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: PBO increases by $500,000; plan assets decrease by $500,000

questions.explanation: Benefit payments reduce both the PBO and plan assets by the same amount. When benefits are paid to retirees, the obligation to those retirees is satisfied (reducing PBO), and the cash leaves the plan trust (reducing plan assets). Therefore, benefit payments have no effect on the funded status (PBO − Plan Assets). Answer is A.
questions.explanationJa: 給付支払はPBOと制度資産の両方を同額減少させる。退職者に給付が支払われると、その退職者に対する債務が消滅し（PBO減少）、制度信託から現金が流出する（制度資産減少）。したがって、給付支払は積立状況（PBO − 制度資産）に影響しない。

questions.subtopic: Employer Contribution - Effect on Funded Status
questions.stem: On December 31, 2025, an employer contributes $1,200,000 to its defined benefit pension plan. Before the contribution, the PBO was $10,000,000 and plan assets were $8,000,000. What is the funded status after the contribution?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Underfunded by $800,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Underfunded by $2,000,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Overfunded by $1,200,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Underfunded by $1,200,000

questions.explanation: Before contribution: Funded status = Plan assets − PBO = $8,000,000 − $10,000,000 = ($2,000,000) underfunded. Employer contributions increase plan assets but do not change PBO. After contribution: Plan assets = $8,000,000 + $1,200,000 = $9,200,000. Funded status = $9,200,000 − $10,000,000 = ($800,000) underfunded. Answer is A.

questions.subtopic: Net Gain/Loss Amortization - Average Remaining Service Life
questions.stem: At January 1, 2025, a company's pension plan has an accumulated net actuarial loss in AOCI of $450,000. The PBO is $3,000,000 and plan assets are $2,800,000. The average remaining service life of active employees is 10 years. Using the corridor approach, what amount of net loss is amortized to pension expense in 2025?
questions.explanation: Step 1: Determine the corridor threshold = 10% × max(PBO, Plan Assets) = 10% × max($3,000,000, $2,800,000) = 10% × $3,000,000 = $300,000. Step 2: Excess over corridor = $450,000 − $300,000 = $150,000. Step 3: Amortization = Excess / Average remaining service life = $150,000 / 10 = $15,000. The minimum amortization of net loss for 2025 is $15,000. Answer is A.

questions.stem: On January 1, 2025, a company amends its pension plan, creating prior service cost of $600,000. The plan has 120 active employees, and the average remaining service period is 15 years. Using straight-line amortization over the average remaining service period, what is the annual amortization of prior service cost?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-30-35-11, prior service cost is amortized over the future service periods of active employees expected to receive benefits. When using the straight-line method over the average remaining service period: Annual amortization = $600,000 / 15 years = $40,000 per year. This is an acceptable alternative to the more detailed years-of-service method. Answer is A.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-30-35-11により、過去勤務費用は給付を受ける見込みの現役従業員の将来勤務期間にわたり償却する。平均残存勤務期間にわたる定額法を使用する場合：年間償却額 = $600,000 / 15年 = $40,000。これはより詳細な勤務年数法の代替として認められている。

questions.subtopic: Pension Funded Status - Balance Sheet Classification
questions.stem: An employer sponsors two separate defined benefit pension plans. Plan A is overfunded by $500,000, and Plan B is underfunded by $300,000. How should these be reported on the employer's balance sheet?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Net asset of $200,000 in noncurrent assets

choices.label: B
choices.text: Asset of $500,000 in noncurrent assets and liability of $300,000 in noncurrent liabilities

choices.label: C
choices.text: Liability of $300,000 only; the asset is not recognized

choices.label: D
choices.text: Netted as a single $200,000 asset or liability depending on the company's policy

questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-30-45-1 and ASC 715-30-45-2, an employer that sponsors multiple defined benefit plans must recognize each plan's funded status separately on the balance sheet. Overfunded plans are reported as noncurrent assets, and underfunded plans are reported as liabilities (with current and noncurrent portions if applicable). Netting across separate plans is prohibited. Plan A: $500,000 noncurrent asset. Plan B: $300,000 noncurrent liability. Answer is B.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-30-45-1および45-2により、複数の確定給付制度を有する雇用主は各制度の積立状況を個別にBSに認識する。過剰積立制度は非流動資産、積立不足制度は負債として報告する。異なる制度間の相殺は禁止。制度A：$500,000の非流動資産。制度B：$300,000の非流動負債。

questions.stem: A company has been accounting for its other postretirement benefits (OPEB) on a pay-as-you-go basis. The company now must apply ASC 715-60. Which of the following best describes the accounting impact upon adoption?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The accumulated postretirement benefit obligation (APBO) must be recognized, creating a liability on the balance sheet

choices.label: B
choices.text: No change is required until the next plan amendment

choices.label: C
choices.text: Only future benefit payments need to be accrued going forward

choices.label: D
choices.text: The APBO is recognized as an intangible asset

questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-60, employers must recognize the funded status of postretirement benefit plans on the balance sheet, which is the difference between the APBO and the fair value of any plan assets. When transitioning from pay-as-you-go (cash basis) to accrual accounting under ASC 715-60, the company must recognize the full APBO as a liability. Since OPEB plans are typically unfunded, this creates a significant liability. Answer is A.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-60により、雇用主は退職後給付制度の積立状況をBSに認識しなければならない。従量制（現金主義）からASC 715-60の発生主義に移行する場合、APBO全額を負債として認識する必要がある。OPEB制度は通常積立されていないため、多額の負債が計上される。

questions.stem: A company sponsors an OPEB plan providing health benefits to retirees. At December 31, 2025, the expected postretirement benefit obligation (EPBO) for an employee aged 55 is $200,000. The employee was hired at age 30, and the full eligibility date is age 60. What is the APBO attributable to this employee at December 31, 2025?
questions.explanation: The APBO is the portion of the EPBO attributed to employee service to date. Under ASC 715-60-35-5, the attribution period runs from the hire date (age 30) to the full eligibility date (age 60), a total of 30 years. Years of service to date = age 55 − age 30 = 25 years. APBO = EPBO × (years of service / total attribution period) = $200,000 × (25/30) = $200,000 × 0.83333 = $166,667. Answer is A.
questions.explanationJa: APBOは従業員の勤務実績に帰属するEPBOの部分である。ASC 715-60-35-5により、帰属期間は入社日（30歳）から完全受給適格日（60歳）までの30年間。勤務年数 = 55歳 − 30歳 = 25年。APBO = EPBO × (勤務年数 / 帰属期間) = $200,000 × (25/30) = $166,667。

questions.stem: Under ASC 715-60, which of the following best describes the expected postretirement benefit obligation (EPBO)?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The actuarial present value of all benefits expected to be paid to the employee and dependents after retirement

choices.label: B
choices.text: The portion of expected benefits attributed to service rendered to date

choices.label: C
choices.text: The amount of benefits actually paid in the current year

choices.label: D
choices.text: The fair value of plan assets set aside for postretirement benefits

questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-60-20, the EPBO is the actuarial present value of all postretirement benefits expected to be paid to an employee, the employee's beneficiaries, and covered dependents. It represents the full estimated cost of benefits from the expected retirement date through the end of coverage. The APBO (choice B) is the portion of the EPBO attributed to service to date. Answer is A.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-60-20により、EPBOは従業員、受益者、被扶養者に支払われると予想される全退職後給付の数理計算上の現在価値である。予想退職日からカバレッジ終了までの給付の全推定コストを表す。APBO（選択肢B）はEPBOのうち現在までの勤務に帰属する部分である。

questions.stem: An employer estimates that the per capita claims cost for retiree health benefits in 2025 is $8,000. The health care cost trend rate is 7% for 2026, declining by 0.5% per year to an ultimate rate of 5%. What is the estimated per capita claims cost for 2027?
questions.explanation: The health care cost trend rate for 2026 is 7%, and for 2027 it is 6.5% (7% minus 0.5% decline). Per capita claims cost for 2026 = $8,000 × 1.07 = $8,560. Per capita claims cost for 2027 = $8,560 × 1.065 = $9,116.40, rounded to $9,116. Answer is A.

questions.subtopic: Defined Benefit Plan - Cash Balance Plan
questions.stem: A cash balance pension plan provides participants with a hypothetical account balance that grows with annual pay credits and interest credits. Under ASC 715, how is a cash balance plan classified?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a defined contribution plan because each participant has an individual account balance

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a defined benefit plan because the employer bears the investment risk

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a hybrid plan that is not subject to ASC 715

choices.label: D
choices.text: As either defined benefit or defined contribution at the employer's election

questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-30-20, a cash balance plan is classified as a defined benefit plan. Although participants receive individual account statements showing hypothetical balances, the employer guarantees a specific rate of return (the interest credit rate) and pools plan assets, retaining the investment risk. The hypothetical account balance is a communication tool; the underlying plan structure places investment risk on the employer, meeting the definition of a defined benefit plan. Answer is B.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-30-20により、キャッシュバランスプランは確定給付制度に分類される。参加者は仮想口座残高の明細を受け取るが、雇用主が特定の利率（利息クレジット率）を保証し、制度資産をプールして投資リスクを負担する。仮想口座残高はコミュニケーションツールであり、基礎となる制度構造は投資リスクを雇用主に課しており、確定給付制度の定義を満たす。

questions.subtopic: Defined Benefit vs Defined Contribution Comparison
questions.stem: Which of the following is a key distinction between defined benefit and defined contribution pension plans under ASC 715?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Defined benefit plans specify the employer's contribution; defined contribution plans specify the retirement benefit

choices.label: B
choices.text: In defined benefit plans, the employer bears the investment risk; in defined contribution plans, the employee bears the investment risk

choices.label: C
choices.text: Only defined contribution plans are required to be funded

choices.label: D
choices.text: Defined benefit plans are accounted for under ASC 718, not ASC 715

questions.explanation: The fundamental distinction is who bears the investment and actuarial risk. In a defined benefit plan (ASC 715-30), the employer promises a determinable retirement benefit and bears the risk that plan assets may not be sufficient. In a defined contribution plan (ASC 715-70), the employer's obligation is limited to the specified contribution; the employee bears the investment risk on the account balance. Choice A reverses the definitions. Answer is B.
questions.explanationJa: 根本的な違いは投資リスクと数理計算上のリスクの負担者である。確定給付制度（ASC 715-30）では、雇用主が確定した退職給付を約束し、制度資産が不足するリスクを負う。確定拠出制度（ASC 715-70）では、雇用主の義務は定められた拠出に限定され、従業員が口座残高の投資リスクを負う。選択肢Aは定義が逆である。

questions.stem: During 2025, a company discovers that the census data used by its actuary incorrectly excluded 50 employees who are active participants in the pension plan. The correction increases the PBO by $2,000,000. How should this correction be accounted for?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a prior period adjustment to retained earnings

choices.label: B
choices.text: As an actuarial loss recognized in other comprehensive income

choices.label: C
choices.text: As additional service cost in the income statement

choices.label: D
choices.text: As a plan amendment creating prior service cost

questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-30-35-14, changes in the PBO resulting from differences between actual experience and actuarial assumptions, including corrections to participant data, are treated as actuarial gains or losses. The $2,000,000 increase in PBO is recognized as an actuarial loss in OCI. It is not a plan amendment (no change to the plan terms) and not a prior period error (it reflects a change in actuarial estimates). Answer is B.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-30-35-14により、実際の経験と数理計算上の仮定の差異（参加者データの修正を含む）によるPBOの変動は数理計算上の損益として扱われる。PBOの$2,000,000増加はOCIに数理計算上の損失として認識される。制度条項の変更がないため制度改定ではなく、数理見積りの変更を反映するものであり過年度の誤謬でもない。

questions.stem: How should net periodic pension cost be recognized in interim financial statements under ASC 270?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The full annual pension cost should be recognized in the first interim period

choices.label: B
choices.text: Pension cost is recognized only at year-end

choices.label: C
choices.text: An aliquot portion (proportional share) of the estimated annual pension cost should be recognized each interim period

choices.label: D
choices.text: Pension cost is recognized only when employer contributions are made

questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-30-35-62 and ASC 270-10-45-7, net periodic pension cost should be recognized ratably across interim periods. The estimated annual pension cost is divided proportionally across the interim periods (for quarterly reporting, one-quarter of the annual cost per quarter). If estimates change during the year, the remaining cost is allocated over the remaining periods. Answer is C.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-30-35-62およびASC 270-10-45-7により、正味期間年金費用は中間期間にわたり均等に認識する。推定年間年金費用を中間期間に比例配分する（四半期報告の場合、年間費用の4分の1を各四半期に計上）。年度中に見積りが変更された場合、残余費用を残りの期間に配分する。

questions.stem: A company purchases a nonparticipating annuity contract from an insurance company for $4,000,000 to settle a portion of its pension obligation. The PBO attributable to the settled participants is $4,200,000, and the unrecognized net loss in AOCI attributable to the settled portion is $300,000. What gain or loss is recognized in the income statement from this settlement?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-30-35-79 through 35-83, a settlement gain or loss includes both the difference between the settlement cost and the PBO settled, and the proportionate share of unrecognized items in AOCI. Difference on settlement: PBO settled − Settlement cost = $4,200,000 − $4,000,000 = $200,000 gain. Plus reclassification of AOCI net loss: $300,000 loss. Net settlement result = $200,000 gain − $300,000 loss = $100,000 loss. Answer is B.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-30-35-79〜83により、決済損益には決済コストと決済されたPBOの差額、およびAOCIの未認識項目の比例配分額が含まれる。決済差額：決済PBO $4,200,000 − 決済コスト $4,000,000 = $200,000の利得。AOCI純損失の組替：$300,000の損失。純決済結果 = $200,000利得 − $300,000損失 = $100,000の損失。

questions.stem: A company closes a factory, eliminating future service for 200 pension plan participants. The unrecognized prior service cost in AOCI attributable to these employees is $800,000. Under ASC 715, how is this prior service cost treated upon curtailment?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Continue amortizing over the original remaining service period

choices.label: B
choices.text: Recognize the $800,000 as a curtailment loss in the income statement

choices.label: C
choices.text: Transfer the $800,000 to goodwill

choices.label: D
choices.text: Recognize the $800,000 as a gain in OCI

questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-30-35-92, when a curtailment eliminates future service for a significant number of employees, the unrecognized prior service cost associated with years of service no longer expected to be rendered is recognized as a curtailment loss. Since the 200 employees will no longer earn future service, the $800,000 of unrecognized prior service cost attributable to them is immediately recognized as a loss in the income statement. Answer is B.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-30-35-92により、カーテイルメントが相当数の従業員の将来勤務を排除する場合、提供されなくなる勤務年数に関連する未認識過去勤務費用はカーテイルメント損失として認識される。200名の従業員が将来勤務を提供しなくなるため、帰属する$800,000の未認識過去勤務費用は損益計算書に即時損失認識される。

questions.stem: Which of the following disclosures is required for an employer participating in a multi-employer pension plan under ASC 715-80?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The employer's proportionate share of the plan's PBO and plan assets

choices.label: B
choices.text: The funded status of the multi-employer plan based on the most recent available information and the employer's contributions

choices.label: C
choices.text: A complete actuarial valuation of the employer's share of the plan

choices.label: D
choices.text: The expected return on the employer's allocated portion of plan assets

questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-80-50-2 through 50-4, an employer participating in a multi-employer plan must disclose, among other items: the plan's funded status (using the most recently available information such as the plan's Form 5500), the employer's total contributions, the nature and effect of any surcharge obligations, and whether the plan is in critical or endangered status. The employer does not separately calculate its proportionate share of PBO or plan assets. Answer is B.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-80-50-2〜50-4により、複数事業主制度に参加する雇用主は、制度の積立状況（Form 5500等の直近入手可能情報）、雇用主の拠出総額、追加負担金の内容と影響、制度が重大または危機的状態にあるかなどを開示する。雇用主はPBOや制度資産の比例配分を個別計算しない。

questions.stem: Under ASC 715, which of the following is an appropriate fair value measurement for pension plan assets?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Plan assets are measured at historical cost less accumulated depreciation

choices.label: B
choices.text: Plan assets are measured at the lower of cost or market

choices.label: C
choices.text: Plan assets are measured at fair value as defined by ASC 820

choices.label: D
choices.text: Plan assets are measured at net realizable value

questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-30-35-59, plan assets used in the funded status calculation must be measured at fair value as of the measurement date, applying the fair value hierarchy in ASC 820 (Level 1, 2, or 3 inputs). Employers must also disclose plan assets by major category (equity securities, debt securities, real estate, etc.) and the level within the fair value hierarchy. Answer is C.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-30-35-59により、積立状況の計算に用いる制度資産は測定日の公正価値で測定し、ASC 820の公正価値ヒエラルキー（レベル1, 2, 3）を適用する。雇用主は制度資産を主要カテゴリー（株式、債券、不動産等）別および公正価値ヒエラルキーのレベル別に開示しなければならない。

questions.subtopic: Projected vs Accumulated Benefit - Key Differences
questions.stem: How does the projected benefit obligation (PBO) differ from the accumulated benefit obligation (ABO)?

choices.label: A
choices.text: PBO includes the effect of projected future salary increases; ABO is based on current salary levels

choices.label: B
choices.text: PBO uses a higher discount rate than ABO

choices.label: C
choices.text: ABO includes projected future salary increases; PBO does not

choices.label: D
choices.text: PBO and ABO are identical for all types of pension plans

questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-30-20, the PBO measures the actuarial present value of all benefits attributed to employee service to date, using assumptions about future salary levels (for pay-related plans). The ABO measures the same obligation but based on current salary levels only, without projecting future salary increases. For flat-benefit plans (not based on salary), PBO and ABO are the same. The PBO is used for pension expense calculations and funded status reporting. Answer is A.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-30-20により、PBOは将来の昇給を仮定して現在までの勤務に帰属する全給付の数理計算上の現在価値を測定する（給与連動型制度の場合）。ABOは将来の昇給を考慮せず、現在の給与水準のみに基づく同様の債務を測定する。定額給付制度（給与に基づかない）ではPBOとABOは同一。PBOは年金費用計算と積立状況の報告に使用される。
