questions.stem: Under ASU 2017-07, a company reports service cost of $500,000 and the following other components: interest cost of $400,000, expected return on plan assets of $(350,000), and amortization of prior service cost of $50,000. What total amount is reported outside of operating income?
questions.explanation: Under ASU 2017-07 (ASC 715-30-45-3A), only service cost is reported in operating income. All other components are reported outside operating income. Components outside operating income: Interest cost $400,000 + Expected return on plan assets ($350,000) + Amortization of prior service cost $50,000 = $400,000 − $350,000 + $50,000 = $100,000. The $500,000 service cost is in operating income. Answer is A.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2017-07（ASC 715-30-45-3A）により、勤務費用のみが営業利益に含まれる。その他の構成要素はすべて営業利益の外に報告される。営業外項目：利息費用$400,000 + 期待収益$(350,000) + 過去勤務費用償却$50,000 = $400,000 − $350,000 + $50,000 = $100,000。勤務費用$500,000は営業利益に含まれる。

questions.subtopic: Plan Freeze Effect on Service Cost
questions.stem: A company freezes its defined benefit pension plan effective January 1, 2025, so that no additional benefits accrue for future service. Which component of net periodic pension cost is most directly affected by the plan freeze?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Interest cost

choices.label: B
choices.text: Service cost

choices.label: C
choices.text: Expected return on plan assets

choices.label: D
choices.text: Amortization of net loss

questions.explanation: A plan freeze eliminates future benefit accruals, which means service cost drops to zero after the freeze date. Service cost represents the actuarial present value of benefits earned by employees during the current period. When no additional benefits accrue, there is no service cost. Interest cost continues because the existing PBO still accrues interest. Expected return on plan assets continues based on asset balances. Amortization of net loss continues based on existing balances in AOCI. Answer is B.
questions.explanationJa: 制度凍結は将来の給付発生を排除するため、凍結日以降の勤務費用はゼロになる。勤務費用は当期に従業員が稼得した給付の数理計算上の現在価値を表す。追加給付が発生しなければ勤務費用はない。利息費用は既存PBOに利息が発生するため継続する。期待収益は資産残高に基づき継続。純損失の償却はAOCIの既存残高に基づき継続する。

questions.stem: On September 30, 2025, a company offers lump-sum payments to vested former employees to settle their pension benefits. Total lump sums paid are $3,500,000, and the settlement eliminates 40% of the PBO. Under ASC 715, what remeasurement is required?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Remeasure plan assets and PBO at September 30 before recognizing the settlement

choices.label: B
choices.text: No remeasurement is needed; use the beginning-of-year balances

choices.label: C
choices.text: Remeasure only the plan assets at September 30

choices.label: D
choices.text: Remeasure only the PBO at September 30

questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-30-35-80 and ASC 715-30-35-67, a settlement requires remeasurement of both the PBO and plan assets at the settlement date (September 30, 2025). This interim remeasurement ensures that the settlement gain or loss is calculated using current values. After remeasurement, the proportionate share of unrecognized items in AOCI (40% in this case) is reclassified to income as part of the settlement gain or loss. Answer is A.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-30-35-80および35-67により、決済は決済日（2025年9月30日）にPBOと制度資産の両方の再測定を要求する。この中間再測定により、決済損益が現在の値で計算される。再測定後、AOCIの未認識項目の比例配分額（本件では40%）が決済損益の一部として損益に組替えられる。

questions.stem: A company elects to immediately recognize all actuarial gains and losses in pension expense rather than using the corridor approach. Under ASC 715, is this election permitted?

choices.label: A
choices.text: No, the corridor approach is the only acceptable method

choices.label: B
choices.text: Yes, as an alternative accounting policy that must be applied consistently and results in faster recognition

choices.label: C
choices.text: Yes, but only for OPEB plans, not pension plans

choices.label: D
choices.text: Yes, but only if the actuarial gain or loss exceeds 20% of the larger of PBO or plan assets

questions.explanation: ASC 715-30-35-25 states that the corridor method establishes minimum amortization, but an employer may adopt any systematic method that results in faster recognition, provided it is applied consistently. Immediate recognition of all actuarial gains and losses in the income statement (rather than deferring in AOCI) is a permitted policy election. Many companies have adopted this approach for greater transparency. This must be disclosed as an accounting policy. Answer is B.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-30-35-25は、回廊法が最低償却額を定めるが、一貫して適用される限り、より早い認識をもたらす体系的な方法を採用できると規定する。全数理計算上の損益をAOCIに繰延べず損益計算書で即時認識することは許容される方針選択である。多くの企業が透明性向上のためこの方法を採用している。会計方針として開示が必要である。

questions.stem: An employer sponsors three defined benefit plans. Plan X is overfunded by $1,000,000, Plan Y is underfunded by $600,000, and Plan Z is underfunded by $400,000. What amounts are reported on the balance sheet?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Noncurrent asset of $1,000,000 and noncurrent liability of $1,000,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: No amounts reported because the net funded status is zero

choices.label: C
choices.text: Noncurrent asset of $400,000 and noncurrent liability of $400,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Noncurrent liability of $1,000,000 only

questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-30-45-2, each plan's funded status must be recognized separately. Overfunded and underfunded plans cannot be netted against each other. Plan X: Noncurrent asset of $1,000,000. Plan Y: Noncurrent liability of $600,000. Plan Z: Noncurrent liability of $400,000. Total noncurrent liability = $600,000 + $400,000 = $1,000,000. The balance sheet reports a noncurrent asset of $1,000,000 and a noncurrent liability of $1,000,000. Answer is A.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-30-45-2により、各制度の積立状況は個別に認識する。過剰積立と積立不足の制度は相殺できない。制度X：非流動資産$1,000,000。制度Y：非流動負債$600,000。制度Z：非流動負債$400,000。非流動負債合計 = $600,000 + $400,000 = $1,000,000。BSには非流動資産$1,000,000と非流動負債$1,000,000を計上。

questions.stem: Which of the following is a required pension disclosure under ASC 715-20-50?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Expected benefit payments for each of the next five years and in the aggregate for the following five years

choices.label: B
choices.text: Expected benefit payments for the next year only

choices.label: C
choices.text: Expected benefit payments for the remaining life of the plan

choices.label: D
choices.text: Expected employer contributions for the next 20 years

questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-20-50-1(g), employers must disclose the expected benefit payments for each of the next five fiscal years individually, and in the aggregate for the five years thereafter (years 6-10). This disclosure helps financial statement users assess the timing of future cash flows from the plan. Additionally, employers must disclose expected contributions for the next fiscal year. Answer is A.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-20-50-1(g)により、雇用主は次の5会計年度それぞれの予想給付支払額を個別に、さらにその後5年間（6〜10年目）の合計額を開示しなければならない。この開示は財務諸表利用者が制度からの将来キャッシュフローのタイミングを評価するのに役立つ。また、翌会計年度の予想拠出額も開示が必要。

questions.stem: Under ASC 715-60, how should an employer account for the federal subsidy received under Medicare Part D for providing prescription drug coverage to retirees that is actuarially equivalent to Medicare Part D?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The subsidy reduces the APBO and net periodic postretirement benefit cost

choices.label: B
choices.text: The subsidy is recognized as other income, separate from pension accounting

choices.label: C
choices.text: The subsidy is added to plan assets

choices.label: D
choices.text: The subsidy has no effect on the financial statements until received in cash

questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-60-35-155 through 35-158, the effects of the Medicare Part D subsidy are to be accounted for as an actuarial gain that reduces the APBO and as a reduction of net periodic postretirement benefit cost (primarily through reduced service cost and interest cost). The subsidy represents a reduction in the employer's expected cost of providing retiree prescription drug benefits and is reflected in the measurement of the obligation. Answer is A.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-60-35-155〜158により、メディケアパートD補助金の効果はAPBOを減少させる数理計算上の利得として、また正味期間退職後給付費用の減少（主に勤務費用と利息費用の減少）として会計処理される。補助金は退職者処方薬給付の予想コスト減少を表し、債務の測定に反映される。

questions.stem: Which of the following is a significant difference between ASC 715 (US GAAP) and IAS 19 (IFRS) for defined benefit pension plans?

choices.label: A
choices.text: IAS 19 uses net interest on the net defined benefit liability/asset; ASC 715 uses separate interest cost on PBO and expected return on assets

choices.label: B
choices.text: IAS 19 does not require recognition of funded status on the balance sheet

choices.label: C
choices.text: ASC 715 requires immediate recognition of all remeasurements in profit or loss

choices.label: D
choices.text: IAS 19 uses the corridor approach for actuarial gains and losses

questions.explanation: A key difference between ASC 715 and IAS 19 (revised 2011) is the treatment of the return on plan assets. Under ASC 715, expected return on plan assets is a separate component of pension expense. Under IAS 19, there is no expected return concept; instead, net interest is calculated by applying the discount rate to the net defined benefit liability (or asset), combining interest cost on the obligation and return on assets into a single net amount. IAS 19 eliminated the corridor approach in 2011 (choice D is wrong). Both require balance sheet recognition (choice B is wrong). Answer is A.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715とIAS 19（2011年改訂）の主要な差異は制度資産のリターンの扱いである。ASC 715では制度資産の期待収益が年金費用の独立した構成要素。IAS 19では期待収益の概念はなく、純確定給付負債（または資産）に割引率を適用して純利息を計算し、債務の利息と資産のリターンを単一の純額に統合する。IAS 19は2011年に回廊法を廃止した（選択肢Dは誤り）。両基準ともBS認識を要求する（選択肢Bは誤り）。

questions.subtopic: Net Periodic Pension Cost - Transition Asset Credit
questions.stem: Borealis Corp. reports the following for the current year: service cost $120,000; interest cost $90,000; expected return on plan assets $110,000; amortization of net loss $8,000; and amortization of a net transition asset (credit) $5,000. What is the net periodic pension cost?
questions.explanation: Net periodic pension cost = service cost + interest cost - expected return + amortization of net loss - amortization of transition asset. A net transition asset is amortized as a credit (reduction) to pension cost. NPPC = $120,000 + $90,000 - $110,000 + $8,000 - $5,000 = $103,000. The transition asset reduces cost while the net loss amortization increases it.
questions.explanationJa: 正味期間年金費用 = 勤務費用 + 利息費用 - 期待収益 + 純損失償却 - 移行時資産償却。純移行時資産は年金費用の貸方（減額）として償却する。NPPC = $120,000 + $90,000 - $110,000 + $8,000 - $5,000 = $103,000。移行時資産は費用を減少させ、純損失償却は増加させる。

questions.subtopic: Net Gain Amortization - Corridor with Gain Balance
questions.stem: At January 1, Caldera Corp. has an accumulated net actuarial GAIN in AOCI of $700,000. The PBO is $4,500,000 and the market-related value of plan assets is $5,000,000. The average remaining service period of active employees is 10 years. What is the amount of net gain amortized that REDUCES the current year's net periodic pension cost?
questions.explanation: The corridor equals 10% of the greater of the PBO ($4,500,000) or the market-related value of plan assets ($5,000,000), which is 10% × $5,000,000 = $500,000. The accumulated net gain of $700,000 exceeds the corridor by $200,000. Minimum amortization = $200,000 / 10 years = $20,000. Because it is a net gain, this $20,000 amortization reduces net periodic pension cost.
questions.explanationJa: 回廊 = max(PBO $4,500,000, 市場関連価値 $5,000,000) × 10% = $500,000。累積純利得$700,000は回廊を$200,000超過する。最低償却額 = $200,000 / 10年 = $20,000。純利得であるため、この$20,000の償却は正味期間年金費用を減少させる。

questions.subtopic: Prior Service Cost - Years-of-Service Method Unequal Lives
questions.stem: Dunmore Corp. amends its plan on January 1, creating prior service cost of $90,000. Three employees benefit: Employee 1 will work 1 more year, Employee 2 will work 2 more years, and Employee 3 will work 3 more years (total service-years = 6). Using the years-of-service amortization method, what is the prior service cost amortization in Year 1?
questions.explanation: The years-of-service method allocates prior service cost based on the total service-years rendered each period. Total future service-years = 1 + 2 + 3 = 6. In Year 1, all three employees render service (3 service-years). Fraction for Year 1 = 3/6. Amortization = $90,000 × (3/6) = $45,000. The amount declines in later years as employees leave.
questions.explanationJa: 勤務年数法は各期に提供される総勤務年数に基づき過去勤務費用を配分する。総将来勤務年数 = 1 + 2 + 3 = 6。第1年は3人全員が勤務する（3勤務年数）。第1年の比率 = 3/6。償却額 = $90,000 × (3/6) = $45,000。従業員が退職するにつれ後年は減少する。

questions.subtopic: Net Periodic Pension Cost - Solve for Service Cost
questions.stem: Eastvale Corp. reports net periodic pension cost of $145,000. The other known components are: interest cost $80,000; expected return on plan assets $70,000; amortization of prior service cost $12,000; and amortization of net loss $18,000. What is the service cost component?
questions.explanation: Net periodic pension cost = service cost + interest cost - expected return + PSC amortization + net loss amortization. Rearranging: service cost = NPPC - interest cost + expected return - PSC amortization - net loss amortization = $145,000 - $80,000 + $70,000 - $12,000 - $18,000 = $105,000.
questions.explanationJa: 正味期間年金費用 = 勤務費用 + 利息費用 - 期待収益 + 過去勤務費用償却 + 純損失償却。整理すると：勤務費用 = NPPC - 利息費用 + 期待収益 - 過去勤務費用償却 - 純損失償却 = $145,000 - $80,000 + $70,000 - $12,000 - $18,000 = $105,000。

questions.subtopic: Funded Status - Reconciliation to Recognized Liability
questions.stem: Foxglove Corp. has the following at December 31: PBO $6,200,000; fair value of plan assets $5,400,000; unrecognized prior service cost (debit) in AOCI $300,000; unrecognized net loss (debit) in AOCI $450,000. What net pension liability is recognized on the December 31 balance sheet?
questions.explanation: ASC 715 requires the balance sheet to report only the funded status: fair value of plan assets minus PBO. The unrecognized prior service cost and net loss remain in accumulated OCI within equity and are not netted into the recognized pension liability. Net pension liability = PBO $6,200,000 - plan assets $5,400,000 = $800,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715は貸借対照表に積立状況（制度資産の公正価値 - PBO）のみを報告することを求める。未認識の過去勤務費用と純損失は資本内のAOCIに残り、認識する年金負債には相殺しない。年金純負債 = PBO $6,200,000 - 制度資産 $5,400,000 = $800,000。

questions.stem: Greenbriar Corp. makes lump-sum payments during the year to settle vested benefits. Its service cost for the year is $150,000 and interest cost is $130,000. The cumulative settlement amount (lump sums paid) is $260,000. Under the ASC 715 settlement guidance, may the employer elect NOT to recognize a settlement gain or loss?

choices.label: A
choices.text: No, any settlement always requires gain or loss recognition

choices.label: B
choices.text: Yes, because lump-sum payments are never settlements

choices.label: C
choices.text: Yes, because the settlement amount ($260,000) does not exceed the sum of service cost and interest cost ($280,000)

choices.label: D
choices.text: No, because the settlement amount exceeds service cost alone

questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-30-35-82, if the cost of all settlements in a year is less than or equal to the sum of the service cost and interest cost components for that year, gain or loss recognition is permitted but not required (the employer may elect a consistent policy). Here the settlement cost of $260,000 is less than service cost plus interest cost of $150,000 + $130,000 = $280,000, so recognition is optional.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-30-35-82により、年間の全清算コストがその年の勤務費用と利息費用の合計以下である場合、損益認識は容認されるが要求されない（雇用主は一貫した方針を選択できる）。ここでは清算コスト$260,000が勤務費用＋利息費用 $150,000 + $130,000 = $280,000を下回るため、認識は任意である。

questions.subtopic: Curtailment - Loss When PSC Exceeds PBO Gain
questions.stem: Hollowmere Corp. terminates a business segment, eliminating future service for affected employees. The curtailment reduces the PBO by $250,000. The unrecognized prior service cost (debit) attributable to the affected employees is $370,000. What net curtailment gain or loss is recognized in earnings?
questions.explanation: A curtailment requires immediate recognition of the unrecognized prior service cost related to the eliminated future service ($370,000 loss) and recognition of the change in PBO ($250,000 gain from the reduction). Net effect = $250,000 gain - $370,000 loss = $120,000 net loss recognized in earnings.
questions.explanationJa: 縮小では、排除された将来勤務に関連する未認識過去勤務費用（$370,000の損失）と、PBOの変動（減少による$250,000の利得）を即時認識する。正味効果 = $250,000利得 - $370,000損失 = $120,000の純損失を損益に認識する。

questions.stem: Ironwood Corp. sponsors an OPEB plan. For an employee, the expected postretirement benefit obligation (EPBO) is $360,000. The employee was hired at age 25, becomes fully eligible at age 65, and is currently age 49. What is the accumulated postretirement benefit obligation (APBO) attributed to this employee at the current measurement date?
questions.explanation: The attribution period runs from the date of hire (age 25) to the full eligibility date (age 65), totaling 40 years. Service rendered to date = age 49 - age 25 = 24 years. APBO = EPBO × (service to date / attribution period) = $360,000 × (24/40) = $360,000 × 0.60 = $216,000.
questions.explanationJa: 帰属期間は入社日（25歳）から完全受給適格日（65歳）までの40年間。現在までの勤務年数 = 49歳 - 25歳 = 24年。APBO = EPBO × (現在までの勤務 / 帰属期間) = $360,000 × (24/40) = $360,000 × 0.60 = $216,000。

questions.subtopic: OPEB - Net Periodic Postretirement Benefit Cost
questions.stem: Juniper Corp.'s OPEB plan reports the following for the year: service cost $40,000; interest cost on the APBO $52,000; the plan is unfunded (no plan assets); amortization of prior service cost $9,000; and amortization of net loss $6,000. What is the net periodic postretirement benefit cost?
questions.explanation: Because the OPEB plan is unfunded, there is no expected return on plan assets to deduct. Net periodic postretirement benefit cost = service cost + interest cost + amortization of prior service cost + amortization of net loss = $40,000 + $52,000 + $9,000 + $6,000 = $107,000.
questions.explanationJa: OPEB制度は未積立であるため、控除すべき制度資産の期待収益はない。正味期間退職後給付費用 = 勤務費用 + 利息費用 + 過去勤務費用償却 + 純損失償却 = $40,000 + $52,000 + $9,000 + $6,000 = $107,000。

questions.subtopic: Total OCI Impact - Combined Gains and Amortization
questions.stem: Kestrel Corp. has the following pension activity for the year recognized in OCI: a current-year actuarial loss arising on the PBO of $90,000; an asset gain (actual return exceeding expected return) of $35,000; amortization of net loss out of AOCI to expense of $12,000; and amortization of prior service cost out of AOCI to expense of $8,000. What is the net change in accumulated OCI (pretax) for the year?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $90,000 increase in net loss (debit)

choices.label: B
choices.text: $55,000 decrease in net loss (credit)

choices.label: C
choices.text: $35,000 increase in net loss (debit)

choices.label: D
choices.text: $125,000 increase in net loss (debit)

questions.explanation: New losses increase the AOCI debit balance, while asset gains and amortizations to expense reduce it. Change = current actuarial loss $90,000 - asset gain $35,000 - net loss amortization $12,000 - prior service cost amortization $8,000 = $35,000 net increase in the accumulated loss (debit) position. The new actuarial loss outweighs the gain and the amortizations released to earnings.
questions.explanationJa: 新規損失はAOCIの借方残高を増加させ、資産利得と費用への償却は減少させる。変動 = 当期数理損失 $90,000 - 資産利得 $35,000 - 純損失償却 $12,000 - 過去勤務費用償却 $8,000 = $35,000の累積損失（借方）の純増。新規数理損失が利得および損益に振り替えた償却額を上回る。

questions.subtopic: Plan Assets Rollforward - Solve for Actual Return
questions.stem: Larkspur Corp.'s pension plan shows: fair value of plan assets at January 1 $2,400,000; fair value at December 31 $2,760,000; employer contributions $300,000; and benefits paid $180,000. There were no administrative expenses paid from the plan. What was the actual return on plan assets for the year?
questions.explanation: Ending plan assets = beginning + actual return + contributions - benefits paid. Solving for actual return: actual return = ending - beginning - contributions + benefits paid = $2,760,000 - $2,400,000 - $300,000 + $180,000 = $240,000.
questions.explanationJa: 期末制度資産 = 期首 + 実際収益 + 拠出金 - 給付支払。実際収益について解くと：実際収益 = 期末 - 期首 - 拠出金 + 給付支払 = $2,760,000 - $2,400,000 - $300,000 + $180,000 = $240,000。

questions.stem: Marsh & Vale Corp. has a projected benefit obligation of $4,800,000 at the beginning of the year. The expected long-term rate of return on plan assets is 8%, the discount rate is 5.5%, and the assumed compensation increase rate is 3%. What is the interest cost component of net periodic pension cost?
questions.explanation: Interest cost is computed by applying the discount rate (the settlement rate), not the expected return rate or the compensation increase rate, to the beginning-of-year PBO. Interest cost = $4,800,000 × 5.5% = $264,000. The 8% expected return rate applies to plan assets, and the 3% rate applies to future salary projections in measuring the PBO.
questions.explanationJa: 利息費用は、期待収益率や昇給率ではなく割引率（決済利率）を期首PBOに適用して計算する。利息費用 = $4,800,000 × 5.5% = $264,000。8%の期待収益率は制度資産に、3%の率はPBO測定における将来給与予測に適用される。

questions.subtopic: Net Periodic Pension Cost - Expected Return from MRVA
questions.stem: Northgate Corp. uses a market-related value of plan assets of $3,000,000 (the fair value is $3,250,000). The expected long-term rate of return is 9% and the discount rate is 6%. Service cost is $200,000 and interest cost is $150,000. Ignoring amortizations, what is net periodic pension cost?
questions.explanation: The expected return is based on the market-related value of plan assets, not the fair value. Expected return = $3,000,000 × 9% = $270,000. Net periodic pension cost = service cost + interest cost - expected return = $200,000 + $150,000 - $270,000 = $80,000.
questions.explanationJa: 期待収益は公正価値ではなく制度資産の市場関連価値に基づく。期待収益 = $3,000,000 × 9% = $270,000。正味期間年金費用 = 勤務費用 + 利息費用 - 期待収益 = $200,000 + $150,000 - $270,000 = $80,000。

questions.subtopic: Market-Related Value of Plan Assets - Definition
questions.stem: Under ASC 715, the market-related value of plan assets used to compute the expected return on plan assets may be:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Only the fair value at the measurement date

choices.label: B
choices.text: Either fair value or a calculated value that recognizes changes in fair value systematically over a period not exceeding five years

choices.label: C
choices.text: The original cost of the plan assets

choices.label: D
choices.text: The fair value averaged over the prior ten years

questions.explanation: ASC 715-30-35-47 defines market-related value as either fair value or a calculated value that recognizes changes in fair value in a systematic and rational manner over a period not exceeding five years. This smoothing technique dampens the volatility of the expected return component. Different methods may be used for different classes of plan assets, but each must be applied consistently.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-30-35-47は、市場関連価値を公正価値、または5年を超えない期間にわたり公正価値の変動を体系的かつ合理的に認識する計算値のいずれかと定義する。この平準化手法は期待収益の構成要素のボラティリティを抑える。資産クラスごとに異なる方法を用いることもできるが、各々を一貫して適用しなければならない。

questions.stem: Under ASU 2017-07, which statement about capitalizing the components of net periodic pension cost into inventory or self-constructed assets is correct?

choices.label: A
choices.text: All components of net periodic pension cost are eligible for capitalization

choices.label: B
choices.text: No component of net periodic pension cost may be capitalized

choices.label: C
choices.text: Only the service cost component is eligible for capitalization into assets such as inventory or self-constructed property

choices.label: D
choices.text: Only interest cost is eligible for capitalization

questions.explanation: Under ASU 2017-07 (ASC 715-30-25-7), only the service cost component is eligible for capitalization in assets such as inventory or self-constructed fixed assets, when capitalization of employee compensation is otherwise appropriate. The other components (interest cost, expected return, amortizations) must be presented outside operating income and are not capitalizable.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2017-07（ASC 715-30-25-7）により、従業員報酬の資産化が他の点で適切である場合、棚卸資産や自家建設固定資産などへの資産化の対象となるのは勤務費用の構成要素のみである。その他の構成要素（利息費用、期待収益、償却）は営業利益の外に表示され、資産化できない。

questions.subtopic: Funded Status Change - Solve for Ending Status
questions.stem: Oakhurst Corp. begins the year with a net pension liability (underfunded status) of $500,000. During the year, plan assets increased by $620,000 (return plus contributions, net of benefits) and the PBO increased by a net $440,000. What is the net funded status reported at year-end?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Underfunded by $320,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Overfunded by $180,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Underfunded by $680,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Underfunded by $120,000

questions.explanation: The funded status equals plan assets minus PBO; a net liability of $500,000 means PBO exceeds assets by $500,000. During the year, assets grew by $620,000 and the PBO grew by $440,000, so the gap narrowed by $620,000 - $440,000 = $180,000. Ending underfunded status = $500,000 - $180,000 = $320,000 net liability.
questions.explanationJa: 積立状況は制度資産 - PBOに等しく、純負債$500,000はPBOが資産を$500,000上回ることを意味する。年度中、資産は$620,000、PBOは$440,000増加したため、差額は$620,000 - $440,000 = $180,000縮小した。期末の積立不足 = $500,000 - $180,000 = $320,000の純負債。

questions.subtopic: Net Loss Amortization - No Amortization Within Corridor
questions.stem: Pinecrest Corp. has an accumulated net actuarial loss in AOCI of $360,000 at the beginning of the year. The PBO is $4,000,000 and the market-related value of plan assets is $3,800,000. The average remaining service period is 8 years. What is the minimum required amortization of the net loss for the year?
questions.explanation: The corridor equals 10% of the greater of the PBO ($4,000,000) or the market-related value of plan assets ($3,800,000), which is 10% × $4,000,000 = $400,000. The accumulated net loss of $360,000 is within (does not exceed) the $400,000 corridor, so no amortization is required. The minimum amortization is $0.
questions.explanationJa: 回廊 = max(PBO $4,000,000, 市場関連価値 $3,800,000) × 10% = $400,000。累積純損失$360,000は回廊$400,000の範囲内（超過しない）であるため、償却は不要である。最低償却額は$0。

questions.stem: Quarrydale Corp. sponsors a defined contribution plan requiring a contribution of 4% of annual eligible compensation. Eligible compensation for the year is $9,000,000. By year-end, the company had funded $300,000 of the required amount. What pension expense and year-end accrued liability should the company report?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Expense $300,000; accrued liability $0

choices.label: B
choices.text: Expense $360,000; accrued liability $60,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Expense $360,000; accrued liability $360,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Expense $60,000; accrued liability $60,000

questions.explanation: For a defined contribution plan, expense equals the required contribution for the period. Required contribution = $9,000,000 × 4% = $360,000, which is the pension expense. Because only $300,000 was funded, an accrued liability of $360,000 - $300,000 = $60,000 is reported for the unpaid portion.
questions.explanationJa: 確定拠出制度では、費用はその期間の必要拠出額に等しい。必要拠出額 = $9,000,000 × 4% = $360,000であり、これが年金費用となる。$300,000のみ拠出されたため、未払分について$360,000 - $300,000 = $60,000の未払負債を計上する。

questions.stem: Riverton Corp. recognizes a current-year actuarial loss of $200,000 in other comprehensive income related to its pension plan. The company's enacted tax rate is 25%. What net-of-tax amount is reported in other comprehensive income for this actuarial loss?
questions.explanation: Items recognized in other comprehensive income are reported net of related tax effects. The pretax actuarial loss of $200,000 generates a deferred tax benefit of $200,000 × 25% = $50,000. Net-of-tax OCI loss = $200,000 - $50,000 = $150,000.
questions.explanationJa: その他の包括利益に認識される項目は、関連する税効果を控除して報告する。税引前数理損失$200,000は$200,000 × 25% = $50,000の繰延税金便益を生じる。税引後OCI損失 = $200,000 - $50,000 = $150,000。

questions.subtopic: Pension Journal Entry - Funding Less Than Expense
questions.stem: Sablewood Corp. reports net periodic pension cost of $250,000 for the year and contributes $190,000 to the plan trust. No amounts are recognized in OCI for the year. What is the effect of these transactions on the net pension liability (assuming a beginning underfunded status)?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The net pension liability decreases by $60,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: The net pension liability increases by $250,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: The net pension liability increases by $60,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: The net pension liability decreases by $190,000

questions.explanation: Pension expense increases the net pension liability, while employer contributions (cash paid into the trust) decrease it. With no OCI items, the net change in the liability = pension expense - contributions = $250,000 - $190,000 = $60,000 increase. The journal entry debits pension expense $250,000, credits cash $190,000, and credits the net pension liability $60,000.
questions.explanationJa: 年金費用は年金純負債を増加させ、雇用主拠出金（信託への現金拠出）は減少させる。OCI項目がない場合、負債の純変動 = 年金費用 - 拠出金 = $250,000 - $190,000 = $60,000の増加。仕訳は年金費用$250,000を借方、現金$190,000を貸方、年金純負債$60,000を貸方とする。

questions.subtopic: Net Periodic Pension Cost - Both PSC and Net Gain Amortization
questions.stem: Thornfield Corp. reports: service cost $180,000; interest cost $95,000; expected return on plan assets $130,000; amortization of prior service cost $20,000; and amortization of a net GAIN of $14,000. What is the net periodic pension cost?
questions.explanation: Amortization of prior service cost increases pension cost, while amortization of a net gain decreases it. NPPC = service cost + interest cost - expected return + PSC amortization - net gain amortization = $180,000 + $95,000 - $130,000 + $20,000 - $14,000 = $151,000.
questions.explanationJa: 過去勤務費用の償却は年金費用を増加させ、純利得の償却は減少させる。NPPC = 勤務費用 + 利息費用 - 期待収益 + 過去勤務費用償却 - 純利得償却 = $180,000 + $95,000 - $130,000 + $20,000 - $14,000 = $151,000。

questions.stem: Under ASC 715-60, over what period is the expected postretirement benefit obligation (EPBO) attributed to arrive at the accumulated postretirement benefit obligation (APBO)?

choices.label: A
choices.text: From the date of hire to the date the employee is fully eligible for benefits

choices.label: B
choices.text: From the date of hire to the expected retirement date

choices.label: C
choices.text: From the date the employee is fully eligible to the expected death date

choices.label: D
choices.text: Over the employee's entire expected remaining life

questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-60-35-50, postretirement benefits are attributed to employee service from the date of hire (or the plan's credited service start) to the full eligibility date — the date the employee has rendered all service required to earn full benefits. The full eligibility date may precede the actual retirement date. Beyond full eligibility, no additional benefit is attributed even if the employee continues working.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-60-35-50により、退職後給付は入社日（または制度の認定勤務開始日）から完全受給適格日（従業員が満額給付を得るのに必要なすべての勤務を提供した日）まで従業員の勤務に帰属させる。完全受給適格日は実際の退職日より前になり得る。完全受給適格後は、従業員が勤務を継続しても追加給付は帰属しない。

questions.stem: Underhill Corp. settles its entire pension obligation. Immediately before settlement, the PBO equals plan assets (the plan is fully funded), and the unrecognized net loss in AOCI is $480,000. The settlement cost equals the PBO. What gain or loss is recognized on the full settlement?
questions.explanation: When a settlement extinguishes the entire PBO, 100% of the unrecognized net gain or loss in AOCI is reclassified to earnings. Since the settlement cost equals the PBO, there is no additional gain or loss from the price of settlement. The entire $480,000 unrecognized net loss is recognized as a settlement loss in earnings.
questions.explanationJa: 清算がPBO全体を消滅させる場合、AOCIの未認識純損益の100%が損益に組み替えられる。清算コストがPBOに等しいため、清算価格による追加の損益はない。未認識純損失$480,000全額が清算損失として損益に認識される。

questions.stem: Vesterly Corp. has a single defined benefit plan with plan assets at fair value of $7,300,000 and a PBO of $6,900,000. AOCI includes an unrecognized net loss (debit) of $250,000. What amount, and in what classification, does Vesterly report for the funded status on its balance sheet?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A noncurrent asset of $650,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: A noncurrent asset of $400,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: A noncurrent liability of $400,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: A noncurrent asset of $150,000

questions.explanation: The funded status is fair value of plan assets minus PBO = $7,300,000 - $6,900,000 = $400,000 overfunded. An overfunded plan is reported as a noncurrent asset; a prepaid pension asset is never classified as current. The unrecognized net loss in AOCI is a component of equity and does not adjust the recognized asset.
questions.explanationJa: 積立状況は制度資産の公正価値 - PBO = $7,300,000 - $6,900,000 = $400,000の過剰積立。過剰積立の制度は非流動資産として報告する。前払年金資産は決して流動に分類しない。AOCIの未認識純損失は資本の構成要素であり、認識する資産を調整しない。

questions.subtopic: Actuarial Gain from Discount Rate Increase
questions.stem: Westmarch Corp. increases its discount rate assumption from 5% to 6% at the measurement date because high-quality bond yields rose. What is the effect on the PBO and how is the change recognized?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The PBO increases, producing an actuarial loss recognized in other comprehensive income

choices.label: B
choices.text: The PBO decreases, producing a gain recognized immediately in net income

choices.label: C
choices.text: The PBO is unaffected because the discount rate only affects interest cost

choices.label: D
choices.text: The PBO decreases, producing an actuarial gain recognized in other comprehensive income

questions.explanation: An increase in the discount rate lowers the present value of future benefit payments, decreasing the PBO. A change in an actuarial assumption that decreases the obligation produces an actuarial gain, which is recognized in other comprehensive income (not immediately in net income) and then subject to corridor amortization. A higher discount rate also raises future interest cost on the lower base, but the immediate remeasurement effect is the gain.
questions.explanationJa: 割引率の上昇は将来給付支払の現在価値を低下させ、PBOを減少させる。債務を減少させる数理計算上の仮定変更は数理計算上の利得を生じ、これは（純利益に即時ではなく）その他の包括利益に認識され、その後回廊償却の対象となる。割引率上昇は低い基礎額に対する将来の利息費用も上昇させるが、即時の再測定効果は利得である。

questions.subtopic: Net Periodic Pension Cost - Full Computation from Raw Data
questions.stem: Yarrowfield Corp. provides: PBO at January 1 $3,600,000; market-related value of plan assets at January 1 $3,000,000; discount rate 5%; expected long-term rate of return 7%; service cost $210,000; amortization of prior service cost $18,000; and amortization of net loss $9,000. What is the net periodic pension cost?
questions.explanation: Interest cost = $3,600,000 × 5% = $180,000. Expected return = $3,000,000 × 7% = $210,000. NPPC = service cost + interest cost - expected return + PSC amortization + net loss amortization = $210,000 + $180,000 - $210,000 + $18,000 + $9,000 = $207,000.

questions.stem: Zephyr Corp.'s OPEB plan covers an employee whose EPBO is $400,000. The employee was hired at age 28 and reaches full eligibility at age 58 (a 30-year attribution period). What is the annual service cost attributed to this employee under the benefit/years-of-service approach, ignoring interest accretion?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-60-35-52, when the plan does not attribute disproportionate benefits to later years, the EPBO is attributed in equal amounts to each year of the attribution period. Annual service cost (before interest) = EPBO / attribution period = $400,000 / 30 years = $13,333. Each year's attribution accumulates toward the APBO.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-60-35-52により、制度が後年に不均衡な給付を帰属させない場合、EPBOは帰属期間の各年に均等額で帰属させる。年間勤務費用（利息前）= EPBO / 帰属期間 = $400,000 / 30年 = $13,333。各年の帰属額がAPBOに向けて累積する。

questions.stem: An employer makes a single lump-sum cash payment to a group of vested participants that irrevocably relieves the employer of the primary responsibility for their pension benefit obligation and eliminates significant risks related to those obligations. Under ASC 715, this transaction is best classified as a:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Curtailment

choices.label: B
choices.text: Plan amendment

choices.label: C
choices.text: Settlement

choices.label: D
choices.text: Negative prior service cost event

questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-30-35-79, a settlement is a transaction that is an irrevocable action, relieves the employer of primary responsibility for a benefit obligation, and eliminates significant risks related to the obligation and the assets used to effect the settlement. Lump-sum cash payments and nonparticipating annuity purchases qualify. A curtailment, by contrast, reduces expected future service; it does not extinguish existing obligations for past service.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-30-35-79により、清算とは、取消不能な行為であり、給付債務に対する主たる責任から雇用主を解放し、その債務および清算に用いる資産に関連する重大なリスクを排除する取引である。一時金の現金支払や非参加型年金の購入が該当する。対照的に縮小は将来の予想勤務を減少させるものであり、過去勤務に対する既存債務を消滅させるものではない。

questions.subtopic: Expected Return - Effect on Accrued/Prepaid Reconciliation
questions.stem: Ashbourne Corp.'s pension plan had a beginning net pension liability of $700,000. For the year, service cost was $160,000, interest cost was $90,000, expected return on plan assets was $120,000, and no amortizations or OCI items occurred. The employer contributed $200,000. What is the ending net pension liability?
questions.explanation: Net periodic pension cost = service cost + interest cost - expected return = $160,000 + $90,000 - $120,000 = $130,000, which increases the liability. Employer contributions of $200,000 decrease the liability. Ending net pension liability = beginning $700,000 + expense $130,000 - contributions $200,000 = $630,000.
questions.explanationJa: 正味期間年金費用 = 勤務費用 + 利息費用 - 期待収益 = $160,000 + $90,000 - $120,000 = $130,000であり、負債を増加させる。雇用主拠出金$200,000は負債を減少させる。期末年金純負債 = 期首$700,000 + 費用$130,000 - 拠出金$200,000 = $630,000。

questions.subtopic: Prior Service Cost - Remaining Unamortized Balance
questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Brackenridge Corp. recognized prior service cost of $480,000 from a plan amendment, amortizing it straight-line over a 12-year average remaining service period. No further amendments occur. What is the unamortized prior service cost remaining in AOCI at December 31, Year 4?
questions.explanation: Annual amortization = $480,000 / 12 years = $40,000. Over four years (Years 1 through 4), cumulative amortization = $40,000 × 4 = $160,000. Remaining unamortized prior service cost in AOCI = $480,000 - $160,000 = $320,000.

questions.subtopic: PBO Rollforward - Solve for Actuarial Gain/Loss
questions.stem: Ridgemont Corp.'s defined benefit pension plan reports the following for the current year: PBO at January 1, $5,200,000; service cost, $310,000; discount rate, 5%; benefits paid to retirees, $420,000; PBO at December 31, $5,500,000. There were no plan amendments during the year. What amount of actuarial gain or loss on the PBO arose during the year?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $150,000 actuarial loss

choices.label: B
choices.text: $150,000 actuarial gain

choices.label: C
choices.text: $270,000 actuarial gain

choices.label: D
choices.text: $410,000 actuarial loss

questions.explanation: The expected ending PBO before any actuarial adjustment is: $5,200,000 beginning PBO + $310,000 service cost + $260,000 interest cost ($5,200,000 × 5%) − $420,000 benefits paid = $5,350,000. Because the actual ending PBO of $5,500,000 exceeds the expected balance by $150,000, an actuarial loss of $150,000 arose during the year. A $150,000 actuarial gain reverses the direction of the difference; the obligation grew larger than expected, not smaller. A $270,000 gain results from incorrectly omitting the benefits paid from the rollforward ($5,770,000 − $5,500,000). A $410,000 loss results from omitting the interest cost component ($5,090,000 expected vs. $5,500,000 actual).
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-30におけるPBOのロールフォワードは「期首PBO＋勤務費用＋利息費用−給付支払±数理計算上の差異＝期末PBO」で構成される。数理差異控除前の期末予想PBOは $5,200,000 + $310,000 + ($5,200,000 × 5% = $260,000) − $420,000 = $5,350,000。実際の期末PBOは $5,500,000 であり、予想を $150,000 上回っているため、当期に $150,000 の数理計算上の損失（PBOの増加）が発生したことになる。給付支払を除外すると $270,000 の利得という誤答に、利息費用を除外すると $410,000 の損失という誤答になる。

questions.stem: A company sponsors a defined benefit pension plan with a benefit formula based on final salary, and some participants are not yet fully vested. Which of the following correctly orders the three measures of the pension obligation from smallest to largest?

choices.label: A
choices.text: ABO, VBO, PBO

choices.label: B
choices.text: VBO, ABO, PBO

choices.label: C
choices.text: PBO, ABO, VBO

choices.label: D
choices.text: VBO, PBO, ABO

questions.explanation: The vested benefit obligation (VBO) includes only benefits that employees are entitled to regardless of future service, so it is the smallest measure. The accumulated benefit obligation (ABO) adds nonvested benefits but uses current salary levels. The projected benefit obligation (PBO) is the largest because it uses the same accrued service as the ABO but incorporates assumptions about future compensation increases, which a final-salary formula makes relevant. Any ordering that places the PBO below the ABO, or the ABO below the VBO, contradicts these definitions when the plan is pay-related and some benefits are nonvested.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-30では年金債務に3つの測定値がある。VBO（受給権確定給付債務）は将来勤務に関係なく確定している給付のみを含むため最小。ABO（累積給付債務）はこれに未確定（nonvested）給付を加えるが、現在の給与水準で測定する。PBO（予測給付債務）はABOと同じ勤務期間を対象としつつ将来の昇給を織り込むため、最終給与比例型の制度では最大となる。したがって小さい順に VBO ≤ ABO ≤ PBO が正しい。

questions.subtopic: Plan Assets Rollforward - Solve for Benefits Paid
questions.stem: Stoneharbor Corp.'s defined benefit pension plan reports the following: fair value of plan assets at January 1, $3,100,000; actual return on plan assets for the year, $280,000; employer contributions during the year, $450,000; fair value of plan assets at December 31, $3,460,000. What amount of benefits was paid to retirees from plan assets during the year?
questions.explanation: The plan assets rollforward is: beginning fair value + actual return + employer contributions − benefits paid = ending fair value. Solving: $3,100,000 + $280,000 + $450,000 − benefits paid = $3,460,000, so benefits paid = $370,000. An answer of $90,000 results from omitting the actual return from the rollforward. An answer of $360,000 is simply the change in fair value ($3,460,000 − $3,100,000), which ignores the inflows and outflows. An answer of $730,000 is the sum of the actual return and contributions, which is the total inflow rather than the benefits paid.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-30における制度資産の公正価値ロールフォワードは「期首公正価値＋実際運用収益＋事業主拠出−給付支払＝期末公正価値」。$3,100,000 + $280,000 + $450,000 − 給付支払 = $3,460,000 を解くと、給付支払 = $370,000。実際運用収益を除外すると $90,000、単純な期中増減額（$3,460,000 − $3,100,000）を答えると $360,000、収益と拠出の合計（流入総額）を答えると $730,000 という誤答になる。

questions.subtopic: Corridor Amortization - MRVA Greater Than PBO
questions.stem: At January 1, Tidewater Corp.'s defined benefit pension plan has an accumulated net actuarial loss in AOCI of $980,000. The PBO at that date is $8,400,000, and the market-related value of plan assets is $9,000,000. The average remaining service period of active employees is 8 years. Using the corridor approach, what is the minimum amortization of the net actuarial loss for the year?
questions.explanation: The corridor is 10% of the greater of the beginning-of-year PBO or the market-related value of plan assets. Here the market-related value ($9,000,000) exceeds the PBO ($8,400,000), so the corridor is $900,000. The excess of the net loss over the corridor is $980,000 − $900,000 = $80,000, and the minimum amortization is $80,000 ÷ 8 years = $10,000. An answer of $17,500 results from incorrectly using 10% of the PBO ($840,000) as the corridor, yielding an excess of $140,000 ÷ 8. An answer of $0 ignores that the loss exceeds the corridor. An answer of $122,500 amortizes the entire $980,000 loss over 8 years without applying the corridor.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-30-35-24のコリドー・アプローチでは、コリドーは期首PBOと制度資産の市場関連価値（MRVA）のいずれか大きい方の10%。本問ではMRVA $9,000,000 > PBO $8,400,000 なのでコリドーは $900,000。純損失 $980,000 のうちコリドー超過額は $80,000 で、これを平均残存勤務期間8年で除した $10,000 が最低償却額。PBOの10%（$840,000）を誤って使うと $140,000 ÷ 8 = $17,500、コリドーを適用せず全額を8年償却すると $122,500 という誤答になる。

questions.subtopic: Funded Status - Current vs Noncurrent Classification
questions.stem: Under ASC 715, how should an employer with a classified balance sheet present the current portion of an underfunded defined benefit pension plan's liability?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A current liability is reported to the extent the actuarial present value of benefits expected to be paid within the next 12 months exceeds the fair value of plan assets

choices.label: B
choices.text: The entire net pension liability is always classified as noncurrent because pension obligations are long-term in nature

choices.label: C
choices.text: The liability is allocated between current and noncurrent based on the ratio of retired participants to active participants

choices.label: D
choices.text: A current liability is reported equal to the employer's expected contribution for the next 12 months

questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-20-45, a liability for an underfunded plan is classified as current to the extent that the actuarial present value of benefits included in the obligation payable within the next 12 months exceeds the fair value of the plan's assets, because those near-term payments cannot be covered by existing plan assets. Treating the entire liability as automatically noncurrent ignores this requirement. Allocating by participant ratio has no basis in the guidance. Expected employer contributions are a disclosure item, not the measure of the current liability; contributions go into the plan rather than directly to retirees.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-20-45では、区分式貸借対照表において、過少積立制度の負債は「今後12か月以内に支払われる給付の数理現価が制度資産の公正価値を超える額」を流動負債として表示する。制度資産で賄える範囲の給付支払は事業主の流動負債とならないため、超過分のみが流動となる。年金債務は長期だから全額固定負債とする処理や、退職者比率による按分は根拠がない。翌期の予定拠出額は開示項目であり、流動負債の測定基準ではない。

questions.subtopic: Net Periodic Pension Cost - Five Components with Gain Amortization
questions.stem: Wrenfield Corp. reports the following for the current year: service cost, $240,000; interest cost, $130,000; expected return on plan assets, $160,000; amortization of prior service cost, $25,000; amortization of net actuarial gain, $15,000. What is Wrenfield's net periodic pension cost for the year?
questions.explanation: Net periodic pension cost = service cost $240,000 + interest cost $130,000 − expected return on plan assets $160,000 + amortization of prior service cost $25,000 − amortization of net actuarial gain $15,000 = $220,000. Amortization of a net gain reduces pension cost, while amortization of prior service cost increases it. An answer of $250,000 results from adding the gain amortization instead of subtracting it. An answer of $200,000 results from flipping the signs of both amortization items (subtracting prior service cost and adding the gain). An answer of $210,000 results from ignoring both amortization components.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-30-35における年金費用の5要素の合算: 勤務費用 $240,000 + 利息費用 $130,000 − 期待運用収益 $160,000 + 過去勤務費用償却 $25,000 − 数理計算上の純利得の償却 $15,000 = $220,000。純利得の償却は費用の減額要素、過去勤務費用の償却は増額要素である点に注意。利得償却を加算すると $250,000、両償却の符号を逆にすると $200,000、両償却を無視すると $210,000 という誤答になる。

questions.subtopic: Prior Service Cost - AOCI Balance After First-Year Amortization
questions.stem: On January 1 of the current year, Alderbrook Corp. amends its defined benefit pension plan, granting retroactive benefits that increase the PBO by $350,000. The average remaining service period of employees expected to receive benefits is 10 years, and the company uses straight-line amortization. What balance related to this prior service cost remains in accumulated other comprehensive income at December 31 of the current year?
questions.explanation: The full $350,000 of prior service cost is recognized in other comprehensive income at the date of the amendment and then amortized into net periodic pension cost over the 10-year average remaining service period. The first year's amortization is $350,000 ÷ 10 = $35,000, leaving $350,000 − $35,000 = $315,000 in AOCI at year-end. An answer of $350,000 ignores that amortization begins in the year of the amendment. An answer of $35,000 is the annual amortization amount, not the remaining balance. An answer of $0 would only result from immediate full recognition in expense, which is not how prior service cost is treated under ASC 715.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-30-35では、制度改訂による過去勤務費用 $350,000 は改訂時にその他の包括利益（OCI）に全額計上され、その後、給付を受ける従業員の平均残存勤務期間（10年）にわたり年金費用へ定額償却される。初年度の償却額は $350,000 ÷ 10 = $35,000 なので、期末のAOCI残高は $350,000 − $35,000 = $315,000。改訂年度から償却が始まる点を見落とすと $350,000、年間償却額そのものを答えると $35,000 という誤答になる。

questions.subtopic: Transactions Affecting PBO vs Plan Assets
questions.stem: Which of the following events decreases the projected benefit obligation of a defined benefit pension plan without affecting the fair value of plan assets?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Payment of benefits to retirees from plan assets

choices.label: B
choices.text: An employer cash contribution to the plan

choices.label: C
choices.text: Actual return earned on plan investments

choices.label: D
choices.text: An increase in the discount rate used to measure the obligation

questions.explanation: An increase in the discount rate reduces the present value of future benefit payments, decreasing the PBO and creating an actuarial gain, while plan assets are unaffected because no cash moves. Benefit payments decrease both the PBO and plan assets simultaneously. An employer contribution increases plan assets only and has no effect on the PBO. The actual return on plan investments changes plan assets only; it has no effect on the obligation.
questions.explanationJa: 割引率の引き上げは将来給付の現在価値を低下させるためPBOを減少させ（数理計算上の利得）、現金の移動がないため制度資産には影響しない。給付支払はPBOと制度資産の両方を同額減少させる。事業主拠出は制度資産のみを増加させ、PBOには影響しない。制度資産の実際運用収益は資産側のみの変動であり、債務には影響しない（ASC 715-30-35参照）。

questions.subtopic: Partial Settlement - Pro Rata Gain Recognition
questions.stem: Cravenmoor Corp. purchases nonparticipating annuity contracts to settle 30% of its projected benefit obligation. Immediately before the settlement, the company has an unrecognized net actuarial gain in AOCI of $400,000 related to the plan. What is the maximum settlement gain Cravenmoor may recognize in earnings as a result of this transaction?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-30-35-79, when only part of the obligation is settled, the employer recognizes in earnings a pro rata portion of the net gain or loss in AOCI equal to the percentage reduction in the PBO. Here, 30% × $400,000 = $120,000 is the maximum gain recognized. Recognizing the full $400,000 would be appropriate only for a settlement of the entire obligation. An answer of $280,000 represents the 70% portion that remains in AOCI, not the amount recognized. An answer of $0 would apply only if the settlement did not meet the recognition criteria, which a 30% irrevocable annuity purchase does.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-30-35-79では、債務の一部のみを清算（settlement）した場合、AOCIに含まれる数理計算上の純利得（または損失）のうち、PBOの減少割合に相当する比例部分を損益に認識する。本問では 30% × $400,000 = $120,000 が認識可能な最大の清算利得。全額 $400,000 の認識は債務全体を清算した場合のみ。$280,000 はAOCIに残る70%部分であり、認識額ではない。

questions.subtopic: Curtailment - Net Gain with PSC Write-Off
questions.stem: Driftstone Corp. closes a regional operation, eliminating the expected future services of a significant portion of plan participants. The curtailment decreases the PBO by $150,000. The curtailment also eliminates 40% of the remaining expected future service years on which unamortized prior service cost of $240,000 was being amortized. Ignoring any net actuarial gains or losses in AOCI, what net curtailment gain or loss should Driftstone recognize?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $54,000 net curtailment gain

choices.label: B
choices.text: $150,000 net curtailment gain

choices.label: C
choices.text: $96,000 net curtailment loss

choices.label: D
choices.text: $54,000 net curtailment loss

questions.explanation: A curtailment requires writing off the portion of unamortized prior service cost associated with the eliminated future service years: 40% × $240,000 = $96,000 loss. The decrease in the PBO of $150,000 is a curtailment gain. The net effect is a $150,000 gain − $96,000 loss = $54,000 net curtailment gain. An answer of $150,000 gain ignores the required prior service cost write-off. An answer of $96,000 loss ignores the PBO decrease. A $54,000 net loss reverses the relative sizes of the two components; the PBO gain exceeds the write-off here.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-30-35-92以降の縮小（curtailment）会計では、消滅した将来勤務年数に対応する未償却過去勤務費用を損失として即時認識する: 40% × $240,000 = $96,000。一方、PBOの減少 $150,000 は縮小利得となる。純額では $150,000 − $96,000 = $54,000 の純縮小利得。過去勤務費用の償却切捨てを無視すると $150,000 の利得、PBO減少を無視すると $96,000 の損失という誤答になる。

questions.stem: Emberlyn Corp.'s pension plan has a market-related value of plan assets of $6,000,000 at January 1. The expected long-term rate of return on plan assets is 8%. On July 1, the employer contributes $600,000 to the plan, and the actuary's methodology time-weights contributions in computing the expected return. No benefits were paid during the year. What is the expected return on plan assets for the year?
questions.explanation: The expected return is computed on the beginning market-related value plus a time-weighted adjustment for the mid-year contribution: ($6,000,000 × 8%) + ($600,000 × 8% × 6/12) = $480,000 + $24,000 = $504,000. An answer of $480,000 ignores the contribution entirely. An answer of $528,000 applies a full year of expected return to the July 1 contribution rather than weighting it for the six months it was in the plan. An answer of $456,000 incorrectly subtracts the half-year return on the contribution instead of adding it.
questions.explanationJa: 期待運用収益は期首の市場関連価値に期待収益率を乗じ、期中拠出は在籍期間で加重して加算する: ($6,000,000 × 8%) + ($600,000 × 8% × 6/12) = $480,000 + $24,000 = $504,000。期中拠出を無視すると $480,000、7月1日の拠出に1年分の収益率を適用すると $528,000 という誤答になる（ASC 715-30-35-47参照）。

questions.subtopic: AOCI Net Loss - Ending Balance Rollforward
questions.stem: At January 1, Fallowfield Corp. has an accumulated net actuarial loss in AOCI of $500,000 related to its pension plan. During the year, the company amortizes $40,000 of the net loss into net periodic pension cost, and a new actuarial loss of $120,000 arises at the year-end measurement date. What is the accumulated net actuarial loss in AOCI at December 31?
questions.explanation: The AOCI rollforward is: beginning net loss $500,000 − amortization reclassified into pension cost $40,000 + new loss arising $120,000 = $580,000 ending balance. Amortization is a reclassification out of AOCI into earnings, so it reduces the AOCI balance. An answer of $460,000 omits the new loss ($500,000 − $40,000). An answer of $620,000 adds the amortization to AOCI instead of removing it while omitting the new loss ($500,000 + $120,000) yields the same figure. An answer of $660,000 adds both the amortization and the new loss ($500,000 + $40,000 + $120,000), treating the reclassification as an increase.
questions.explanationJa: AOCIの数理計算上純損失のロールフォワード: 期首残高 $500,000 − 当期償却（年金費用への組替額）$40,000 + 当期発生の新たな損失 $120,000 = 期末残高 $580,000。償却はAOCIから損益への組替（reclassification adjustment）なのでAOCI残高を減少させる。新規損失を無視すると $460,000、償却を加算扱いにすると $660,000 という誤答になる（ASC 715-30-35-24、ASC 220参照）。

questions.subtopic: Disclosure - Plans with ABO in Excess of Plan Assets
questions.stem: An employer sponsors several defined benefit pension plans, some overfunded and some underfunded. Under ASC 715-20-50, which of the following disclosures is required when one or more plans have an accumulated benefit obligation in excess of plan assets?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Only the net aggregate funded status of all plans combined, because individual plan information is never disclosed

choices.label: B
choices.text: The aggregate PBO, aggregate ABO, and aggregate fair value of plan assets for the plans with an ABO in excess of plan assets

choices.label: C
choices.text: The name and individual funded status of each underfunded plan, listed separately

choices.label: D
choices.text: Only a qualitative statement that certain plans are underfunded, with no quantitative information

questions.explanation: ASC 715-20-50 requires that when one or more plans have an ABO in excess of plan assets, the employer disclose the aggregate PBO, aggregate ABO, and aggregate fair value of plan assets for those plans as a group. Disclosing only the combined net funded status of all plans would mask the underfunded plans by offsetting them against overfunded plans. Plan-by-plan disclosure naming each plan is not required; the guidance permits aggregation of the affected plans. A purely qualitative statement does not satisfy the quantitative disclosure requirement.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-20-50では、ABOが制度資産を超過する制度が1つ以上ある場合、当該制度群について、PBO合計・ABO合計・制度資産の公正価値合計を開示することが求められる。全制度を純額合算した積立状況のみの開示では、過大積立制度との相殺により過少積立制度の実態が隠れてしまう。制度ごとの個別名称付き開示までは要求されておらず、該当制度の集計開示で足りる。定性的記述のみでは定量的開示要件を満たさない。

questions.subtopic: Funding in Excess of Pension Cost - Balance Sheet Effect
questions.stem: Glassmere Corp. reports net periodic pension cost of $300,000 for the year and contributes $380,000 in cash to its underfunded defined benefit pension plan. Ignoring any remeasurement gains or losses, what is the effect of these events on Glassmere's net pension liability?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The net pension liability decreases by $80,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: The net pension liability increases by $80,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: The net pension liability decreases by $380,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: The net pension liability is unchanged

questions.explanation: Pension cost (excluding remeasurement effects) increases the net pension liability by $300,000, while the cash contribution increases plan assets and therefore decreases the net liability by $380,000. The net effect is a decrease of $380,000 − $300,000 = $80,000. An increase of $80,000 reverses the direction; funding exceeded cost, so the liability shrinks. A decrease of $380,000 considers only the contribution and ignores the cost recognized for the year. No change would occur only if the contribution exactly equaled the pension cost.
questions.explanationJa: 再測定の影響を除けば、年金費用 $300,000 は純年金負債を増加させ、現金拠出 $380,000 は制度資産を増加させるため純負債を減少させる。純額では $380,000 − $300,000 = $80,000 の負債減少。拠出が費用を上回る（超過拠出）場合、純年金負債は減少する。仕訳イメージ: 年金費用 300,000 / 現金 380,000、差額80,000は純年金負債の借方（減少）。拠出のみを見ると $380,000 減少という誤答になる。

questions.subtopic: Plan Termination - Combined Settlement and Curtailment
questions.stem: Hartwicke Corp. terminates its defined benefit pension plan entirely. Employees immediately stop accruing benefits, and the company purchases nonparticipating annuity contracts that irrevocably transfer the full obligation to an insurance company. How should this plan termination be accounted for under ASC 715?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a settlement only, because the obligation was transferred to the insurer

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a curtailment only, because future benefit accruals ceased

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a plan amendment, with the effects amortized over the remaining service period

choices.label: D
choices.text: As both a curtailment (for the cessation of future accruals) and a settlement (for the irrevocable transfer of the obligation)

questions.explanation: A full plan termination typically involves two distinct events with separate accounting: the cessation of future benefit accruals is a curtailment, which triggers write-off of applicable prior service cost and recognition of curtailment gain or loss; the irrevocable annuity purchase that relieves the employer of primary responsibility for the obligation is a settlement, which triggers recognition of the net gain or loss in AOCI. Treating the transaction as only one of the two events omits required recognition for the other. A plan amendment with prospective amortization applies to benefit formula changes, not to a termination.
questions.explanationJa: 制度の完全終了は通常2つの事象を含み、それぞれ別個に会計処理する。将来の給付発生の停止は縮小（curtailment）に該当し、関連する未償却過去勤務費用の切捨てと縮小損益の認識を要する。買戻不能な年金保険契約の購入による債務の移転は清算（settlement）に該当し、AOCI中の数理計算上純損益の認識を要する（ASC 715-30-35-92、35-79）。いずれか一方のみとして処理すると他方の認識が漏れる。制度改訂としての償却処理は給付算定式の変更に適用されるものであり、終了には妥当しない。

questions.subtopic: Combined Asset and Liability Gains/Losses - Net OCI Effect
questions.stem: During the year, Ivorydale Corp.'s pension plan has an actual return on plan assets of $320,000 against an expected return of $250,000. At the year-end measurement date, a change in actuarial assumptions increases the PBO by $110,000. There is no amortization of existing AOCI amounts. What is the net effect of these two items on the accumulated net actuarial gain or loss in AOCI?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Net loss in AOCI increases by $180,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Net loss in AOCI increases by $110,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Net loss in AOCI increases by $40,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Net loss in AOCI decreases by $40,000

questions.explanation: The asset gain is the excess of actual over expected return: $320,000 − $250,000 = $70,000 gain. The assumption change creates a $110,000 liability loss. Both are recognized in OCI in the period they arise. The net effect is a $110,000 loss − $70,000 gain = $40,000 net increase in the accumulated net loss. An increase of $180,000 adds the two amounts as if both were losses. An increase of $110,000 ignores the offsetting asset gain. A decrease of $40,000 reverses the direction; the liability loss exceeds the asset gain, so the net loss grows.
questions.explanationJa: 資産側の利得は実際運用収益と期待運用収益の差額: $320,000 − $250,000 = $70,000 の利得。数理仮定の変更によるPBO増加 $110,000 は債務側の損失。いずれも発生期にOCIで認識される（ASC 715-30-35-21）。純額では損失 $110,000 − 利得 $70,000 = $40,000 だけAOCIの純損失が増加する。両者を損失として合算すると $180,000、資産利得を無視すると $110,000 という誤答になる。

questions.subtopic: OPEB - APBO and EPBO at Full Eligibility
questions.stem: Under ASC 715-60, what is the relationship between the accumulated postretirement benefit obligation (APBO) and the expected postretirement benefit obligation (EPBO) for an employee who has reached the full eligibility date?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The APBO equals the EPBO, because the full benefit has been attributed to service already rendered

choices.label: B
choices.text: The APBO continues to be less than the EPBO until the employee actually retires

choices.label: C
choices.text: The APBO exceeds the EPBO because interest continues to accrue on the APBO

choices.label: D
choices.text: The APBO is reset to zero and attribution begins again over the remaining service period

questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-60, the EPBO is attributed to service from the date of hire to the full eligibility date. Once an employee reaches full eligibility, the entire EPBO has been attributed, so the APBO equals the EPBO from that point forward and no further service cost is recognized for that employee. The APBO does not remain below the EPBO until retirement; attribution ends at full eligibility, not retirement. Interest accrues on both measures equally, so it does not cause the APBO to exceed the EPBO. There is no resetting of the obligation at full eligibility.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-60では、EPBO（予想退職後給付債務）は入社日から完全受給資格日（full eligibility date）までの勤務期間に帰属（attribution）させる。完全受給資格日に達した時点でEPBOの全額が帰属済みとなるため、それ以降APBOはEPBOと等しくなり、当該従業員について追加の勤務費用は認識されない。年金（退職日まで帰属）と異なり、OPEBの帰属期間は完全受給資格日で終わる点が特徴。利息は両測定値に等しく発生するためAPBOがEPBOを上回ることはない。

questions.subtopic: Net Periodic Pension Cost - Solve for Expected Return
questions.stem: Junewood Corp. reports net periodic pension cost of $210,000 for the year. The components include: service cost, $190,000; interest cost, $85,000; amortization of prior service cost, $20,000. There is no amortization of net actuarial gains or losses. What was the expected return on plan assets for the year?
questions.explanation: Net periodic pension cost = service cost + interest cost − expected return + amortization of prior service cost. Solving for expected return: $190,000 + $85,000 + $20,000 − $210,000 = $85,000. An answer of $65,000 results from omitting the prior service cost amortization from the equation ($190,000 + $85,000 − $210,000). An answer of $105,000 double-counts the prior service cost amortization ($85,000 + $20,000). An answer of $20,000 is simply the amortization component, not the expected return.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-30-35-4の構成式「年金費用 = 勤務費用 + 利息費用 − 期待運用収益 + 過去勤務費用償却」を期待運用収益について解く: $190,000 + $85,000 + $20,000 − $210,000 = $85,000。過去勤務費用償却を式から落とすと $65,000、二重計上すると $105,000 という誤答になる。

questions.stem: A defined benefit pension plan prepares its own financial statements as a reporting entity under ASC 960. Which of the following is reported in the plan's financial statements?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The sponsor's net periodic pension cost for the year

choices.label: B
choices.text: The actuarial present value of accumulated plan benefits and the net assets available for benefits

choices.label: C
choices.text: The projected benefit obligation measured using future salary levels

choices.label: D
choices.text: The funded status as a single net asset or net liability, consistent with the sponsor's balance sheet

questions.explanation: ASC 960 governs the financial statements of the plan itself, which report the net assets available for benefits and the actuarial present value of accumulated plan benefits, along with changes in each. The plan's benefit obligation measure is based on accumulated benefits (generally current and past compensation), not the PBO with future salary projections, which is the employer's measure under ASC 715. Net periodic pension cost is an element of the sponsor's financial statements, not the plan's. The plan does not present a single netted funded status; the obligation information and net assets are presented separately rather than offset.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 960は年金制度自体（プラン）の財務諸表を規律する。プランの財務諸表では「給付に利用可能な純資産（net assets available for benefits）」と「累積制度給付の数理現価（actuarial present value of accumulated plan benefits）」およびそれぞれの増減を報告する。将来昇給を織り込むPBOは事業主側（ASC 715）の測定値であり、プランの測定値ではない。年金費用（NPPC）はスポンサー側の財務諸表項目。プランは積立状況を純額1本で表示せず、純資産と給付債務情報を別個に表示する。

questions.subtopic: OPEB - Net Cost for an Unfunded Plan
questions.stem: Kelsmere Corp. sponsors an unfunded postretirement health care plan (OPEB). For the current year: service cost is $60,000; the APBO at the beginning of the year is $900,000; the discount rate is 6%. The company paid $45,000 of retiree health claims directly during the year. There are no amortization items. What is Kelsmere's net periodic postretirement benefit cost for the year?
questions.explanation: Net periodic postretirement benefit cost = service cost $60,000 + interest cost ($900,000 × 6% = $54,000) − expected return on plan assets ($0, because the plan is unfunded) = $114,000. Benefit payments of $45,000 reduce the APBO but are not a component of periodic cost. An answer of $54,000 is the interest cost alone. An answer of $60,000 is the service cost alone. An answer of $159,000 incorrectly adds the benefit payments to the cost ($114,000 + $45,000).
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-60における退職後給付費用 = 勤務費用 $60,000 + 利息費用（$900,000 × 6% = $54,000）− 期待運用収益 $0（積立のない制度のため）= $114,000。OPEBは積立されないことが多く、その場合期待運用収益はゼロとなる。事業主が直接支払った給付 $45,000 はAPBOを減少させるが、期間費用の構成要素ではない。給付支払を費用に加算すると $159,000 という誤答になる。
